title: Study-Level Automation & Deep Features
text: tlframe resolves settings four tiers, lowest highest priority: Every fr_table() call merges four tiers. configure broadest settings lowest tier override changes. Mental model: SAS parallel: Tier 2 autoexec.sas + PROC TEMPLATE. Tier 3 OPTIONS top program. Tier 4 inline STYLE()= overrides.
code: +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | 4. Per-table verbs         fr_page(font_size = 10)          <- wins  | | 3. Session theme           fr_theme(font_size = 9)                   | | 2. Project config          _tlframe.yml                              | | 1. Package defaults        inst/defaults/_tlframe.yml       <- lowest| +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

title: Study-Level Automation & Deep Features
text: Place _tlframe.yml project root. Every table project inherits settings without R code.

title: Study-Level Automation & Deep Features
code: # _tlframe.yml — place at project root page:   paper: letter                       # letter | a4 | legal   orientation: landscape              # landscape | portrait   margins: [1.0, 0.75, 1.0, 0.75]    # top, right, bottom, left (inches)   font_family: "Courier New"          # Any system font   font_size: 9                        # Points   continuation: "(continued)"         # Appended to titles on page 2+   col_gap: 4                          # Inter-column gap in points  columns:   split: false                        # true = auto-split wide tables  header:   align: ~                            # null = inherit from column   valign: bottom                      # top | middle | bottom   bold: false   span_gap: true                      # Gap between adjacent spans   n_format: "{name}\n(N={n})"         # N-count label template  pagehead:   left: "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"   center: ~   right: "CONFIDENTIAL"   font_size: ~                        # null = inherit page font_size   bold: false  pagefoot:   left: "{program}"   center: ~   right: "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   font_size: ~   bold: false  rules:   hlines: header                      # header | open | booktabs | box | void   vlines: void                        # box | all | inner | void  footnotes:   separator: false                    # true = horizontal line above footnotes   font_size: ~   placement: every                    # every | last (last = PDF only)  spacing:   titles_after: 1                     # Blank lines after titles   footnotes_before: 1                 # Blank lines before footnotes   pagehead_after: 0   pagefoot_before: 0   page_by_after: 1  titles:   align: center                       # left | center | right   bold: false   font_size: ~  tokens:   study: "TFRM-2024-001"   compound: "Zomerane"   cutoff: "15MAR2025"

title: Study-Level Automation & Deep Features
text: study 50+ tables, structure recipes three layers:
code: # Layer 1: Company standard (shared across all studies) company <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_family = "Courier New",           font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"),   fr_hlines("header") )  # Layer 2: Study-specific (shared across all tables in one study) study <- fr_recipe(   fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"),   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001",                          cutoff = "15MAR2025")) )  # Layer 3: Table-type recipes demog_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center",             n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45,                   zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),             format = "{name}\n(N={n})"),   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.",                .separator = FALSE) )  ae_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center",             n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45,                   zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),             format = "{name}\n(N={n})"),   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)"),   fr_footnotes("MedDRA version 26.0.",                "Subjects counted once per SOC and PT.",                .separator = FALSE) )  # Usage: compose layers + add per-table details tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(c(company, study, demog_recipe)) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_render("output/Table_14_1_1.rtf")  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(c(company, study, ae_recipe)) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE by SOC/PT") |>   fr_cols(soc = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt") |>   fr_render("output/Table_14_3_1.rtf")

text: fr_style_if() applies styles based data values render time. Unlike fr_rows_matches() selects rows, fr_style_if() evaluates condition per cell:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_styles(     # Bold all group header rows     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("group", pattern = "^[A-Z]"),       bold = TRUE     ),     # Tint the total column     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#F0F4F8")   ) #> ℹ `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "group". #> ℹ Selector: pattern ^[A-Z].

title: Study-Level Automation & Deep Features
text: multiple styles target cell, last one wins. Order fr_styles() calls broadest specific:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_styles(     # 1. Base: tint total column (broadest)     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#F0F4F8"),     # 2. Bold group headers     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("group", pattern = "^[A-Z]"),       bold = TRUE     ),     # 3. Specific cell override (most specific — wins)     fr_style(rows = 1, cols = "total", bg = "#003366", fg = "#FFFFFF")   ) #> ℹ `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "group". #> ℹ Selector: pattern ^[A-Z].

text: fr_get_*() functions enable programmatic quality checks:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL.", .separator = FALSE) |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE))  # Check title count stopifnot(length(fr_get_titles(spec)) == 2)  # Check footnote content fn <- fr_get_footnotes(spec)[[1]]$content stopifnot(grepl("ADSL", fn))  # Check page defaults pg <- fr_get_page(spec) stopifnot(pg$orientation == "landscape") stopifnot(pg$font_size == 9)  # Check visible columns cols <- fr_get_columns(spec) visible <- names(cols)[vapply(cols, function(x) {   vis <- x$visible   if (is.null(vis)) TRUE else vis }, logical(1))] stopifnot("group" %in% setdiff(names(cols), visible))

text: architecture details, render pipeline, custom backends, sentinel system: vignette("extending-tlframe").

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: tlframe organised three layers across 24 source modules:
code: +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer (10 files)                                                 | | fr_table -> fr_cols -> fr_header -> fr_titles -> ... -> fr_render    | | Each verb: validate -> modify fr_spec -> return fr_spec              | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Infrastructure Layer (9 files)                                       | | classes.R     S3 constructors (fr_spec, fr_col, fr_rule, ...)        | | columns.R     Width estimation, AFM metrics, auto-distribution       | | config.R      YAML discovery, loading, merging                       | | constants.R   fr_env: fonts, paper sizes, presets, colors            | | helpers.R     Error conditions, text normalisation                   | | markup.R      Inline rich text: fr_super() -> sentinel tokens        | | tokens.R      {thepage}, {program}, {datetime} resolution            | | units.R       Twips, half-points, row height calculation             | | validate.R    Type-safe validation helpers                           | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Render Layer (5 files)                                               | | render.R          Dispatch: finalize -> prepare_pages -> backend     | | render-common.R   Shared: cell grid, borders, escaping, colors       | | render-rtf.R      RTF 1.9.1 backend                                  | | render-latex.R    tabularray/XeLaTeX backend                         | | render-pdf.R      PDF wrapper (generates .tex, compiles twice)       | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: Every pipeline verb reads writes single S3 object, fr_spec. key fields:

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: fr_env (constants.R) private package environment holding lookup data mutable state: Key contents: $fonts (family → AFM mapping), $paper_sizes (name → dimensions), $hline_presets (name → rule set), $named_colors (148 CSS colors), $unicode_map (symbol → codepoint), $liberation_fallback (system font → Liberation font).
code: # Font families and their AFM font names names(fr_backends()) #> [1] "format"      "extensions"  "description"  # Paper sizes are stored internally pg <- fr_get_page(tbl_demog |> fr_table()) pg$paper #> [1] "letter"

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: fr_render(spec, path) called, runs three-stage pipeline:
code: fr_spec   |   v finalize_spec()            <- Resolve all deferred computations   |  - Build default columns for unmentioned data columns   |  - Distribute widths (auto/fit/equal/percent)   |  - Resolve N-count header labels   |  - Insert blank_after rows   |  - Apply indent_by offsets   |  - Compute decimal geometry for align="decimal" columns   |   v prepare_pages()            <- Split data into page groups   |  - Split by page_by column values (preserving data order)   |  - Each group: {label, data, n_counts}   |   v calculate_col_panels()     <- Handle wide tables   |  - If .split=TRUE and total width > page: split into panels   |  - Stub columns repeat in each panel   |  - Scale panel widths proportionally   |   v render_<format>()          <- Backend-specific rendering      RTF:   render_rtf(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)      LaTeX: render_latex(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)      PDF:   render_pdf() -> render_latex() + xelatex compilation

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: deferred computations resolved. Key operations: Width distribution: .width = "auto", column widths estimated content using AFM font metrics. total width exceeds printable area, columns scaled proportionally: N-count labels: fr_header(n = ..., format = "{name}\n(N={n})") stores N-counts format template. finalize_spec() resolves final column labels fr_cols() fr_header() can called order. Blank rows: blank_after = "group" inserts empty rows group boundary. group_by auto-implies blank_after column. Decimal geometry: align = "decimal" columns, 15-type stat display engine analyses cell value, detects type (count, float, n/%, CI, p-value, range, etc.), computes space-padded alignment offsets.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(.width = "auto", group = fr_col(visible = FALSE))  # Widths are resolved at render time, not when fr_cols() is called # This allows verb-order independence

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: page_by = "param" set, data split unique(data$param) (preserving original data order, alphabetical). page group rendered separate section.

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: .split = TRUE total column width exceeds printable area, columns distributed across panels. Stub columns repeat every panel. panel’s widths scaled proportionally fill page.

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: tlframe’s backend interface formalised. backend must implement single function signature: Arguments: Return: output file path (character scalar).
code: render_<format>(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
code: # Register a custom backend for HTML output render_html <- function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {   # ... generate HTML ...   writeLines(html, path)   path }  fr_register_backend("html", render_html, extensions = "html")  # Now this works: spec |> fr_render("output.html")

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: render layer provides shared utilities backend can use: build_cell_grid() — Converts data frame matrix rendered cell content, applying inline markup resolution decimal alignment. build_header_cell_grid() — header row, N-count labels already resolved. resolve_borders() — Converts $rules list per-cell border specification (top, bottom, left, right cell). collect_colors() — Scans styles backgrounds build color table (RTF needs \colortbl; LaTeX needs \definecolor preamble). rtf_escape_and_resolve() / latex_escape_and_resolve() — Escape special characters resolve markup sentinels (\x01SUPER:\x02 → {\super } \textsuperscript{}).

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: Inline markup (fr_super(), fr_bold(), etc.) works sentinel token system survives string concatenation: \x01...\x02 sentinel tokens non-printable bytes : Survive paste(), sprintf(), string operations invisible console output resolved per-backend final rendering stage design means markup works titles, footnotes, column labels, data cells — anywhere string accepted.
code: User writes:       "P{fr_super('a')}-value < 0.05"                           ↓ glue evaluation:   "P\x01SUPER:a\x02-value < 0.05"                           ↓ RTF backend:       "P{\super a}-value < 0.05" LaTeX backend:     "P\textsuperscript{a}-value < 0.05"

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: tlframe uses real Adobe Font Metrics accurate column width estimation. Pre-parsed metrics 12 font variants stored R/sysdata.rda: Width ratios illustrate AFM matters: Helvetica ‘M’ = 833 units vs ‘’ = 222 units (3.75x ratio) Courier fixed 600 units characters “Summary Statistics” column Helvetica needs different width Courier, tlframe accounts automatically estimate_col_width() function measures actual text widths (header labels data content) using measure_text_width_twips(), sums per-character AFM widths correct font variant.

code: # render_all.R — run in CI pipeline library(tlframe) fr_config("_tlframe.yml")  metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {   row <- metadata[i, ]   data <- get(row$data_name)   recipe <- readRDS(sprintf("recipes/%s.rds", row$recipe))    data |>     fr_table() |>     fr_apply(recipe) |>     fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1) |>     fr_render(sprintf("output/Table_%s.rtf",                       gsub("\\.", "_", row$table_id))) }

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
text: reproducible PDF rendering containers: Microsoft fonts required container — tlframe’s bundled Liberation fonts provide pixel-compatible rendering Courier New, Arial, Times New Roman.
code: FROM rocker/r-ver:4.3.3  # Install system dependencies RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \     libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev  # Install R packages RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','glue','rlang','tidyselect','vctrs','yaml','stringi'))" RUN R -e "install.packages('remotes'); remotes::install_github('vthanik/tlframe')"  # For PDF output: install TinyTeX RUN R -e "install.packages('tinytex'); tinytex::install_tinytex()"  # Liberation fonts are bundled with tlframe — no Microsoft fonts needed # tlframe auto-detects and uses Liberation as fallback on Linux

title: Architecture & Custom Backends
code: # validate_all.R — run after rendering library(tlframe)  metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) errors <- character(0)  for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {   row <- metadata[i, ]   data <- get(row$data_name)   recipe <- readRDS(sprintf("recipes/%s.rds", row$recipe))    spec <- data |>     fr_table() |>     fr_apply(recipe) |>     fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1)    result <- fr_validate(spec, strict = FALSE)   if (!result$valid) {     errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Table %s: %s",                                  row$table_id,                                  paste(result$messages, collapse = "; ")))   } }  if (length(errors) > 0) {   stop("Validation failures:\n", paste(errors, collapse = "\n")) }

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: tlframe produces regulatory-grade clinical trial tables, listings, figures (TLFs) RTF PDF single specification. describe output look like; tlframe handles rendering. core idea composable pipeline:  Every function takes spec, returns spec. Side effects happen fr_render(). Verb order doesn’t matter — tlframe resolves everything (column widths, N-count labels, page breaks) render time. Key design choices: dplyr dependency. lightweight infrastructure: rlang, cli, vctrs, stringi. RTF PDF one spec. Change file extension; output adapts. Pharma-native defaults. Landscape letter, 9pt Courier, ICH E3–compliant page headers/footers box. Built-datasets. Every example guide runs data shipped package — external files needed. SAS parallel: tlframe replaces PROC REPORT + ODS RTF/PDF. verb maps DEFINE, COMPUTE, ODS statement. pipeline replaces procedural SAS workflow declarative specification.
code: data |> fr_table() |> fr_titles(...) |> fr_cols(...) |> fr_render("out.rtf")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Every pharma table structural components. ’s map tlframe verbs: Every labelled region exactly one verb. Learn 10 verbs, build table.

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: fr_table() wraps data frame fr_spec object. Printing shows spec contains. produce file: ’s complete, valid RTF table — landscape letter, 9pt Courier, columns auto-labelled column names. configuration required. SAS parallel: PROC REPORT DATA=tbl_demog; RUN; ODS RTF destination open. tlframe’s zero-config output idea. PDF output, change extension:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom spec |> fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")) spec |> fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"))

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: argument fr_titles() produces one centered title line. Calling fr_titles() replaces previous titles. SAS parallel: TITLE1 "Table 14.1.1"; TITLE2 "Demographics...";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) fr_get_titles(spec)[[1]]$content #> [1] "Table 14.1.1"

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Individual title lines can styled passing named list instead plain string: Available list fields: content, align ("left", "center", "right"), bold (logical), font_size (numeric). .align .bold arguments set defaults lines:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     list("Sponsor: Acme Pharma", align = "left"),     list("Protocol: TFRM-2024-001", align = "left"),     "Table 14.1.1",     list("Demographics and Baseline Characteristics", bold = TRUE),     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) length(fr_get_titles(spec)) #> [1] 5 spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     .bold = TRUE, .align = "center"   ) fr_get_titles(spec)[[1]]$bold #> [1] TRUE

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Key options: .separator: TRUE (default) draws thin rule footnotes. Pharma convention often sets FALSE. .placement: "bottom" (default) "last". Last-page-footnotes work PDF; RTF repeats footnotes every page. .align: Default "left". SAS parallel: FOOTNOTE1 "Percentages..."; FOOTNOTE2 "MMSE...";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.",     "MMSE = Mini-Mental State Examination.",     .separator = FALSE   ) length(fr_get_footnotes(spec)) #> [1] 2

what: Inline Markup in Titles and Footnotes
title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Use {fr_*()} expressions inside text string rich formatting: Available markup functions:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "{fr_super('a')} Fisher's exact test.",     "{fr_super('b')} Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test.",     "BMI = Body Mass Index (kg/m{fr_super(2)})."   ) fr_get_footnotes(spec)[[1]]$content #> [1] "\001SUPER:a\002 Fisher's exact test."

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: fr_cols() configures columns. named argument column defined fr_col(): fr_col() parameters: SAS parallel: DEFINE col / DISPLAY "Label" STYLE(column)=[width=2.5in just=right];
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "right"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) names(fr_get_columns(spec)) #> [1] "characteristic" "placebo"        "zom_50mg"       "zom_100mg"      #> [5] "total"          "group"

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: .width argument fr_cols() sets global width strategy: pharma tables, "fit" explicit inch widths work best.
code: # Fixed (default): each column uses its fr_col(width=) or 1.5 inches fr_cols(.width = NULL, ...)  # Auto: estimate from content, scale down if wider than page fr_cols(.width = "auto", ...)  # Fit: like auto, but always scales to fill the full page width fr_cols(.width = "fit", ...)  # Equal: divide page equally among columns without explicit widths fr_cols(.width = "equal", ...)  # Percentage: specify each column as a share of printable width fr_cols(col_a = fr_col("A", width = "35%"), col_b = fr_col("B", width = "65%"))

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Clinical tables frequently display mixed statistics — counts, percentages, means, p-values — column. align = "decimal" automatically detects stat type aligns decimal points: engine recognises 10 stat display types: counts, floats, n (%), n/N (%), estimate (spread), estimate (CI), range pairs, integer ranges, p-values, missing values. Cross-type alignment handled automatically.
code: decimal_data <- data.frame(   statistic = c("n", "Mean (SD)", "Median", "Min, Max", "p-value"),   value     = c("45", "68.2 (7.1)", "68.0", "52, 84", "<0.001"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) spec <- decimal_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     statistic = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.5),     value     = fr_col("Value", width = 2.0, align = "decimal")   )

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Columns used grouping logic shown output: .label_fn argument auto-generates labels column names:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) # group column drives blank_after but doesn't appear in rendered output spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     .label_fn = function(x) gsub("_", " ", tools::toTitleCase(x)),     group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) fr_get_columns(spec)$characteristic$label #> [1] "Characteristic"

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: fr_header() controls header row’s appearance. Parameters include bold, align, valign, bg, fg, font_size. SAS parallel: PROC REPORT ... STYLE(header)=[just=center font_weight=bold];
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Clinical trial tables almost always show treatment arm sample sizes column headers. tlframe supports four progressively powerful forms. Form 1: Named numeric vector (common) format template two tokens: name (column label) {n} (count). default format "{name}\n(N={n})". Form 2: Named list (per-group counts page_by) page_by creates separate pages per parameter, N-counts may differ across pages. Pass named list keyed page_by value: Form 3: Function (computed per page group) maximum flexibility, pass function receives page group’s data label: Form 4: "auto" (computed source dataset) simplest option per-group counts. Provide source dataset subject ID column: tlframe counts unique subjects per treatment arm per page_by group automatically. recommended approach vital signs lab tables.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(     n      = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),     format = "{name}\n(N={n})"   ) fr_header(   n = list(     "Systolic BP (mmHg)"  = c(placebo = 42, zom_50mg = 44, zom_100mg = 43),     "Diastolic BP (mmHg)" = c(placebo = 42, zom_50mg = 44, zom_100mg = 43)   ),   format = "{name}\n(N={n})" ) fr_header(   n = function(group_data, group_label) {     c(placebo = sum(group_data$arm == "Placebo"),       active  = sum(group_data$arm == "Active"))   } ) fr_header(   n = "auto",   n_subject = "USUBJID",   n_data = advs )

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook

code: vs_wk24 <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24",                    c("param", "statistic", "placebo_value",                      "zom_50mg_value", "zom_100mg_value")] spec <- vs_wk24 |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(param = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param", page_by_bold = TRUE)

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: group_by keeps groups together pagination (group split across pages). blank_after inserts blank row group visual separation.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: indent_by indents named column’s values 2 characters whenever value differs group header. SOC terms appear flush-left; PTs indented beneath . SAS parallel: DEFINE pt / DISPLAY INDENT=2; ORDER=INTERNAL SOC variable.
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / Preferred Term", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Real tables use multiple row features together:
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / PT", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total    = fr_col("Total")   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt") |>   fr_header(     n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),     bold = TRUE, align = "center"   ) |>   fr_hlines("header")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: record-level listings, fr_listing() provides listing-optimised defaults (8pt font, left-aligned, text wrapping). sort_by repeat_cols arguments control sort order repeated column suppression: Listing defaults vs table defaults: repeat_cols suppresses repeated values treatment arm prints changes — NOREPEAT SAS PROC REPORT. wrap = TRUE option enables automatic text wrapping long cell values, using column’s width determine break points.
code: ae_list <- adae[1:30, c("USUBJID", "ARM", "AEBODSYS",                           "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "AESER")] spec <- ae_list |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7.1", "Adverse Events") |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID  = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.3),     ARM      = fr_col("Treatment", width = 1.2),     AEBODSYS = fr_col("System Organ Class", width = 2.0),     AEDECOD  = fr_col("Preferred Term", width = 1.5),     AESEV    = fr_col("Severity", width = 0.8),     AESER    = fr_col("Serious", width = 0.7)   ) |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("ARM", "USUBJID"),           repeat_cols = "ARM") |>   fr_hlines("header")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: package defaults — need fr_page() overriding . Available paper sizes: "letter", "a4", "legal". Margin formats:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(     orientation = "landscape",     paper       = "letter",     font_family = "Courier New",     font_size   = 9,     margins     = c(top = 1.0, right = 0.75, bottom = 1.0, left = 0.75)   ) pg <- fr_get_page(spec) pg$orientation #> [1] "landscape" pg$font_size #> [1] 9

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Built-tokens (resolved render time): Custom tokens via fr_page(tokens = ...): SAS parallel: Page headers map ODS ESCAPECHAR='~'; TITLE j=l "Protocol..."  j=r "CONFIDENTIAL";. Pagefoot maps FOOTNOTE j=l "&_SASPROGRAMFILE" j=r "Page ^{thispage} ^{lastpage}";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001",     right = "CONFIDENTIAL"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left   = "{program}",     center = "{datetime}",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   ) spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "15MAR2025")) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}"   )

text: Presets (common first): Custom styling: Width options: "hairline" (0.25pt), "thin" (0.5pt), "medium" (1pt), "thick" (1.5pt), numeric value points. SAS parallel: STYLE(header)=[borderbottomstyle=solid borderbottomwidth=1pt];
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("open", width = "thick", color = "#003366", linestyle = "dashed")

code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("inner") spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_grid("box/all", width = "thin", color = "#999999")

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Spanning headers group columns shared label: multi-level spans, call fr_spans() multiple times different .level values: Unlike verbs, fr_spans() appends repeated calls (doesn’t replace).
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_spans(     "Active Treatment" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) |>   fr_header(n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)) |>   fr_hlines("header") spec |>   fr_spans("Placebo" = c("pbo_base", "pbo_value"), .level = 1) |>   fr_spans("Treatment Group" = c("pbo_base", "pbo_value",                                   "act_base", "act_value"), .level = 2)

title: The Pharma Programmer's Handbook
text: Apply visual overrides specific rows, columns, cells: Style constructors: Like fr_spans(), fr_styles() appends repeated calls. Content-based row selection fr_rows_matches(): fr_rows_matches() also supports regex patterns: Header region styling: Zebra striping:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE),                    # bold first row     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB"),           # tint total column     fr_style(rows = 3, cols = "placebo", fg = "#CC0000")    # red single cell   ) spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / PT", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total    = fr_col("Total")   ) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "soc"),       bold = TRUE     ),     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "total"),       bold = TRUE, bg = "#D5E8D4"     )   ) # Bold all rows where statistic starts with an uppercase letter spec <- tbl_tte |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("statistic", pattern = "^[A-Z]"),       bold = TRUE     )   ) spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, bg = "#003366",              fg = "#FFFFFF", align = "center")   ) spec <- tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = seq(1, nrow(tbl_disp), 2), bg = "#F5F5F5")   )

text: format auto-detected file extension. Override format =: fr_render() returns output path invisibly, making pipeline-friendly: PDF requirements: PDF rendering compiles LaTeX via XeLaTeX. Install tinytex::install_tinytex(). Linux without Microsoft fonts, tlframe automatically uses bundled Liberation fonts fallback.
code: # RTF output spec |> fr_render("output/table_14_1_5.rtf")  # PDF output (requires XeLaTeX via tinytex) spec |> fr_render("output/table_14_1_5.pdf")  # LaTeX source (for manual compilation) spec |> fr_render("output/table_14_1_5.tex") spec |> fr_render("output.txt", format = "rtf") tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))

title: Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE) — adae
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Adverse Events (ADAE) dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per adverse event. Used : AE SOC/PT table (Table 14.3.1): incidence body system preferred term, within treatment arm Overall AE summary: subjects TEAEs seriousness, relatedness, severity, action taken adverse events dataset treatment-emergent (TRTEMFL = "Y"). title: Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE) — adae

Derived AESEV: MILD maps 1–2, MODERATE 2–3, SEVERE 3–4 AESER Serious adverse event flag: "Y" "N" AEREL Relationship study drug: "PROBABLE", "POSSIBLE", "REMOTE", "NONE" AEACN Action taken study treatment: "DOSE CHANGED", "DRUG INTERRUPTED", "DRUG WITHDRAWN", "DOSE REDUCED", "APPLICABLE" AEOUT Outcome: "RECOVERED/RESOLVED" "RECOVERED/RESOLVED" ASTDT AE start date AENDT AE end date ASTDY AE start day relative treatment start AENDY AE end day relative treatment start ADURN AE duration days TRTEMFL Treatment-emergent flag ("Y" records) AESEQ AE sequence number within subject AOCCFL First-occurrence flag (AE, per subject): "Y" NA AOCCSFL First SOC occurrence flag (per subject per SOC): "Y" NA AOCCPFL First PT occurrence flag (per subject per PT): "Y" NA

text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Time--Event (ADTTE) dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per time--event parameter. Used : Time event table (Table 14.2.1): Kaplan-Meier estimates, median time--event, hazard ratios treatment arm Two parameters included: "TTWD" — Time Study Withdrawal (event = discontinued, censored = completed) "TTAE" — Time First Adverse Event (event = first AE, censored = AE)

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: Defines display properties single table column: label, width, alignment, visibility. Used inside fr_cols() named argument configure individual columns. Columns explicitly configured receive auto-generated defaults .width, .align, .label_fn arguments fr_cols().

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
code: fr_col(   label = "",   width = NULL,   align = NULL,   header_align = NULL,   visible = NULL,   stub = FALSE )

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: label Character scalar. Display label shown column header. Supports {fr_*()} inline markup (e.g. "{fr_super('')}" superscript footnote markers). Default "" inherits data frame column name label, optionally transformed .label_fn argument fr_cols(). width Column width. Accepts: Numeric — fixed width inches (e.g. 2.5). Use stub columns need exact control. "auto" — auto-calculate content header widths using page font metrics. layout engine measures widest cell value header label, adds padding, converts inches. Columns proportionally scaled fit printable page width. fastest way get working table. Percentage string — e.g. "20%". Sets column width fraction printable page width. Resolved absolute inches render time. Must "0%" (exclusive) "100%" (inclusive). Useful responsive layouts adapt different page sizes orientations. NULL (default) — inherits .width argument fr_cols(). .width also NULL, package default 1.5 used. align Column alignment. One : "left" — left-aligned. Standard text stub columns. "center" — centered. Rare regulatory tables; used binary indicators (Yes/) short categorical values. "right" — right-aligned. Standard numeric columns (counts, percentages, p-values). "decimal" — decimal-point alignment. Aligns decimal point (last digit integers) across rows column. Standard continuous summary statistics (mean, SD, median). NULL (default) — auto-detects R column type: numeric/integer → "right", everything else → "left". header_align Horizontal alignment column header cell. One "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL (default). NULL, header inherits alignment align. Use center treatment-arm headers keeping body cells right-aligned. Can also set uniformly via fr_header(align = ...); per-column values take priority. fr_style(region = "header", align = ...) overrides . visible Logical NULL. Controls whether column appears rendered output. NULL (default): system decides — columns used fr_rows() page_by keys auto-hidden; others visible. TRUE: force column visible, even page_by key. FALSE: hide column. Useful structural columns (grouping keys, sort-order columns, row-type flags) remain data pagination styling logic appear output. stub Logical. Whether column stub column — repeated every panel fr_cols(.split = TRUE) splits table across multiple column pages. Default FALSE. Stub columns typically row-label parameter columns provide context panel. .split enabled columns stub = TRUE, stubs auto-inferred group_by/indent_by columns first column.

text: Standard pharma house styles (Roche, Novartis, Pfizer TFL guides): Stub / parameter column: left-aligned. Count percentage columns: right-aligned decimal-aligned. P-value columns: right-aligned decimal-aligned. Category columns (Yes/, Male/Female): centered left-aligned.

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
code: ## ── Fixed width with explicit alignment ──────────────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = 2.5, align = "left") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [2.50in, left]  ## ── Percentage width: 25% of printable area ──────────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = "25%") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [25%, left]  ## ── Centered header over right-aligned body ──────────────────────────────  fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", width = 1.5, align = "right", header_align = "center") #> <fr_col> "Zomerane 50mg" [1.50in, right]  ## ── Percentage width in a pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = "30%"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg",  width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",           width = "17.5%", align = "right")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 30% left #> placebo "Placebo" 18% right #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50mg" 18% right #> zom_100mg "Zomerane 100mg" 18% right #> total "Total" 18% right #> group "group" 0.90in left #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Auto-width: let the engine measure content ───────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = "auto") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [auto, left]  ## ── Markup in label: superscript unit ────────────────────────────────────  fr_col("BMI (kg/m{fr_super('2')})", width = 1.5, align = "decimal") #> <fr_col> "BMI (kg/m{fr_super('2')})" [1.50in, decimal]  ## ── Decimal alignment for continuous statistics ──────────────────────────  fr_col("Mean (SD)", width = 1.5, align = "decimal") #> <fr_col> "Mean (SD)" [1.50in, decimal]  ## ── Hidden structural column (grouping key for fr_rows) ─────────────────  fr_col(visible = FALSE) #> <fr_col> "" [auto, left]  ## ── Stub column (repeats in every panel during column splitting) ────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = 2.5, stub = TRUE) #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [2.50in, left stub]  ## ── Footnote marker in column header ─────────────────────────────────────  fr_col("Placebo{fr_super('a')}\n(N=45)", width = 1.5, align = "right") #> <fr_col> "Placebo{fr_super('a')} #> (N=45)" [1.50in, right]  ## ── Inside a full fr_cols call ──────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(     n = c(zom_50mg = 45, placebo = 45),     format = "{name}\n(N={n})"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 1.50in right #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50 mg" 1.50in right #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.97in left #> total "total" 0.97in left #> Header: valign=bottom, N-counts

Tidyselect expressions captured unevaluated resolved style applied via fr_styles(). Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), tidyselect::everything(), tidyselect::(), . bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. fg Foreground (text) colour, NULL. bg Background (fill) colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. valign Vertical alignment: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model.

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: Column styles broadest scope overridden row styles cell styles:   Column alignment set via fr_col(align = ...) fr_cols() base default. fr_col_style(align = ...) overrides body cells. fr_row_style(align = ...) overrides . Finally, fr_style(region = "body", align = ...) targeting specific cells wins.

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: Column alignment usually set fr_cols() via fr_col(align = ...). Use fr_col_style() need override alignment subset columns without reconfiguring full column spec. Use fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB") give Total column distinct background — common regulatory convention. Column styles apply body cells. style header cells, use fr_style(region = "header", cols = ...) fr_header().

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Configures display label, width, alignment, visibility one table columns. fr_cols() owns column structure; fr_header() owns header presentation (alignment, valign, N-counts, bold, colours). Columns explicitly named receive auto-generated defaults: column name label (optionally transformed .label_fn), alignment inferred R column type (numeric → "right", everything else → "left"), width .width. Calling fr_cols() replaces entire column configuration. N-count header labels (e.g. "Placebo\\n(N=45)"), header alignment, header vertical alignment, use fr_header() order-independent fr_cols().

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
code: fr_cols(   spec,   ...,   .list = NULL,   .width = NULL,   .align = NULL,   .label_fn = NULL,   .split = NULL )

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... Column specifications. Can provided two ways: Named arguments: argument name must match column name data frame. value can fr_col() object character scalar (label). Formulas (tidyselect): Use lhs ~ rhs lhs set columns selected via tidyselect::language (e.g., starts_with("col")), rhs fr_col() object character scalar applied selected columns. Powered tidyselect package. data column listed receives auto-generated defaults. .list Named list character vector pre-built labels. Allows construct column labels externally (e.g., using sprintf()) pass programmatically. Example: list(zom_50mg = "Zomerane 50 mg"). .width Default column width applied columns explicit width set. Accepts: Numeric — width inches (e.g. 1.5). Default NULL. "auto" — auto-calculate widths content header text using page font metrics. layout engine measures widest cell value header label column, adds padding, scales total exceeds printable page width. Columns already fit left -. "fit" — like "auto", always scales () total width exactly fills printable page width, preserving content-based ratio columns. Use want every table span full page width without manual tuning. "equal" — distribute printable page width equally among columns fixed width. Columns explicit numeric width fr_col() keep size; remaining space divided equally. Percentage string — e.g. "25%". Sets every column's default width fraction printable page width. Must "0%" (exclusive) "100%" (inclusive). Columns explicit width fr_col() affected. NULL (default) — "auto". Columns auto-size content header text. .align Default alignment applied columns explicit alignment set. One "left", "center", "right", "decimal". NULL auto-detects: "right" numeric columns, "left" everything else. .label_fn function (rlang-style lambda) applied auto-generated column labels. affects columns whose labels explicitly set via ... .list. Receives column name input, returns display label. Common transforms: ~ gsub("_", " ", .x) — replace underscores spaces. ~ tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", .x)) — title case. toupper — caps. .split Logical NULL. Column splitting wide tables exceed printable page width: NULL (default) — splitting. columns one page. TRUE — split across multiple panels, stub columns repeated panel. Panel width behaviour follows .width: .width = "auto" → panels keep natural column widths. .width = "fit" → panels scale fill page width. .width = "equal" → unfixed columns share remaining space equally. FALSE — explicitly splitting. Stub columns (repeated every panel) designated via fr_col(stub = TRUE). .split enabled columns stub = TRUE, stubs auto-inferred group_by/indent_by columns first column.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: modified fr_spec. Column specs stored spec$columns, split settings spec$columns_meta.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Standard pharma house styles FDA/EMA submissions require: Active treatment arm(s) left, order dose escalation (lowest highest dose, left right). Comparator / placebo arm(s) rightmost among study arm columns. Total column (included): optional; covers active arms (pool active + placebo). Placed immediately rightmost active arm column, placebo column. Example column order 2-dose + placebo study:

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Column labels resolved priority (highest wins): fr_header() n / format — dynamic label N counts. Explicit fr_col(label = ...) ... arguments. .list — programmatic label map. .label_fn — transform function applied column name. Column name — raw data frame column name -.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Column order rendered table matches data frame column order, order list fr_cols(). reorder columns, reorder data frame first. Use visible = FALSE suppress column rendering keeping available grouping key fr_rows(). align = "decimal" aligns decimal points numeric columns. Width inches. Landscape Letter 1 margins gives 9 printable width. common pharma layout: stub column 2.5 + 4–5 data columns 1.3–1.5 . .width = "auto" fastest way get working table — layout engine calculates widths content. Use fixed widths need exact control. .label_fn transforms auto-generated labels (column names explicitly relabelled). lets set baseline transform override specific columns needed.

what: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Loads study-level configuration _tlframe.yml file. file path given, auto-discovers nearest _tlframe.yml searching directory tree current working directory. loaded config stored internally automatically applied every subsequent fr_table() call. Config values lowest priority — overridden fr_theme() (session-level) per-table verbs like fr_page() fr_header(). tlframe first pharma TFL package file-driven theming. Place single _tlframe.yml project root every table study inherits fonts, margins, headers, footers, rules without R code.

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
code: fr_config(file = NULL)

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: file Character scalar NULL. Path _tlframe.yml file. NULL (default) triggers auto-discovery: searches directory tree getwd() _tlframe.yml found, falling back package-bundled defaults (inst/defaults/_tlframe.yml).

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Invisibly returns parsed config named list.

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Settings resolved lowest highest priority:   Config _tlframe.yml overrides package defaults. fr_theme() overrides config. Per-table verbs (e.g. fr_page(font_size = 10)) override everything.
code: Package defaults  < _tlframe.yml  < fr_theme()  < per-table verbs

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
code: # _tlframe.yml — place at project root page:   paper: letter   orientation: landscape   margins: [1.0, 0.75, 1.0, 0.75]   # top, right, bottom, left   font_family: "Courier New"   font_size: 9   col_gap: 4  header:   align: ~               # inherit from column (user decides)   valign: bottom   bold: false  pagehead:   left: "{company}"   center: "{study_id}"   right: "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   font_size: 8  pagefoot:   left: "{program}"   right: "{datetime}"   font_size: 8  rules:   hlines: header         # header | booktabs | open | hsides | box | void   vlines: void           # box | all | inner | void  footnotes:   separator: true   font_size: 8  spacing:                 # blank lines between table sections   titles_after: 1        # after titles, before column header   footnotes_before: 1    # after body, before footnotes   pagehead_after: 0      # after page header, before titles   pagefoot_before: 0     # after footnotes, before page footer   page_by_after: 1       # after page_by label, before col headers  tokens:                  # custom token values for pagehead/pagefoot   company: "Pharma Corp"   study_id: "TFRM-2024-001"

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: file = NULL, fr_config() searches _tlframe.yml starting current working directory walking parent directories. mirrors behaviour .Rprofile, _quarto.yml, _pkgdown.yml. typical project layout:
code: study-root/   _tlframe.yml         <-- found here   R/     t_demog.R   output/     tbl_14_1_1.rtf

Create a temporary custom config and load it yml <- file.path(tempdir(), "_tlframe.yml") writeLines(c(   "page:",   "  orientation: landscape",   "  font_size: 8",   "  font_family: 'Courier New'",   "header:",   "  bold: true",   "tokens:",   "  company: 'Pharma Corp'" ), yml) fr_config(yml) fr_config_get()$page$font_size    # 8 #> [1] 8 fr_config_get()$tokens$company    # "Pharma Corp" #> [1] "Pharma Corp"  # Clean up fr_config_reset() unlink(yml)

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
text: named list config settings. Top-level keys mirror YAML structure: page, header, pagehead, pagefoot, rules, footnotes, spacing, tokens. Returns empty list config loaded _tlframe.yml found.

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
code: # Start clean — no config loaded fr_config_reset()  # Load config and inspect top-level keys default_cfg <- system.file("defaults/_tlframe.yml", package = "tlframe") fr_config(default_cfg) cfg <- fr_config_get() names(cfg)                    # page, header, pagehead, etc. #>  [1] "page"      "columns"   "header"    "pagehead"  "pagefoot"  "rules"     #>  [7] "footnotes" "spacing"   "titles"    "tokens"     # Access nested keys cfg$page$font_size            # font size from config #> [1] 9 cfg$page$orientation          # page orientation #> [1] "landscape" cfg$header$bold               # header bold setting #> [1] FALSE  # After reset, fr_config_get() auto-discovers defaults fr_config_reset()  # Load a custom config with specific tokens yml <- file.path(tempdir(), "_tlframe.yml") writeLines(c(   "tokens:",   "  study_id: 'DEMO-001'" ), yml) fr_config(yml) fr_config_get()$tokens$study_id   # "DEMO-001" #> [1] "DEMO-001"  # Clean up fr_config_reset() unlink(yml)

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: Sets one footnote lines displayed table body. argument ... produces one footnote line. Calling fr_footnotes() replaces previously set footnotes. Footnotes support inline {fr_*()} markup, including fr_super() superscript footnote labels.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
code: fr_footnotes(   spec,   ...,   .align = "left",   .placement = "every",   .font_size = NULL,   .separator = FALSE )

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One footnotes. may : character scalar — footnote text. list. first element footnote text (can unnamed). Optionally include align, placement, font_size override formatting. .align Default alignment. "left" (default) standard regulatory submissions. Override per line via list form. .placement print footnotes. "every" (default) repeats footnotes every page; "last" prints final page. Override per footnote via list form. .font_size Default font size points. NULL inherits page. .separator Logical. Whether draw horizontal separator rule footnote block. Default FALSE.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: modified fr_spec. Footnotes stored spec$meta$footnotes.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: ICH E3 common pharma submission standards specify: Labelling: Footnote labels use lower-case letters square brackets: [], [b], [c]. use numeric labels — [1], [2] reserved literature references. begin footnote section word "Note" "Notes:". Layout: footnote line, left-aligned, smaller font table body. RTF/SAS submissions: limit 8 footnotes per table full compatibility standard TLG macro footnote slots. Ordering: [], [b], … content footnotes first (order symbols appear reading left--right, top--bottom table). Abbreviation expansion line last (e.g. "AE = Adverse Event; FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation."). Source / data-cut note final page (placement = "last"). Common regulatory footnote patterns: Percentage denominator: "[] Percentages based N column header." — required whenever n (%) columns appear. MedDRA coding: "[] Coded using MedDRA Version XX.X." — AE, medical history, prior/concomitant medication tables. Subject counting: "[] subject counted multiple events within System Organ Class Preferred Term." — AE tables events > subjects possible. Dagger symbol: use {fr_dagger()} {fr_ddagger()} markup treatment-related serious AE markers.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: default, one blank line inserted last body row first footnote. Control fr_spacing():   can also set _tlframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 0L)   # no gap spec |> fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   footnotes_before: 1

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: Use fr_super() superscript footnote markers inside cell values: "{fr_super('')} Value footnote". matching footnote line begin "[] ...". Set .separator = FALSE table ends bottom rule already visually separates footnotes. placement = "last" source note prevents repeating every continuation page. Note: works PDF output. RTF mechanism last-page-footer content ({\footer} repeats every page). RTF, footnotes appear every page regardless placement. warning emitted rendering RTF placement = "last" footnotes.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
code: ## ── Demographics table: standard footnote set ─────────────────────────────  # Correct order: [a][b] content footnotes, abbreviations last, source final page tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages are based on N in the column header.",     "[b] P-value from two-sample t-test (continuous) or chi-squared (categorical).",     "FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation.",     list("Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (4): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages are based on N in the column header." #> 2. [left] "[b] P-value from two-sample t-test (continuous) or chi-sq..." #> 3. [left] "FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation." #> 4. [left [last]] "Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── AE table: MedDRA + subject-counting + source footnotes ───────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Coded using MedDRA Version 27.1.",     paste0("[b] A subject is counted only once for multiple events within ",            "the same System Organ Class or Preferred Term."),     "[c] Percentages are based on N in the column header.",     paste0("AE = Adverse Event; PT = Preferred Term; ",            "SOC = System Organ Class; TEAE = Treatment-Emergent AE."),     list("Source: ADAE; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (5): #> 1. [left] "[a] Coded using MedDRA Version 27.1." #> 2. [left] "[b] A subject is counted only once for multiple events wi..." #> 3. [left] "[c] Percentages are based on N in the column header." #> 4. [left] "AE = Adverse Event; PT = Preferred Term; SOC = System Org..." #> 5. [left [last]] "Source: ADAE; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── Source note on last page only ────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     list("Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header." #> 2. [left [last]] "Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── Inline markup: dagger and double-dagger symbols ──────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "{fr_dagger()} Treatment-related adverse events.",     "{fr_ddagger()} Serious adverse events."   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "† Treatment-related adverse events." #> 2. [left] "‡ Serious adverse events."  ## ── Per-line font size override ───────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     list("Source: ADSL.", font_size = 7)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header." #> 2. [left] "Source: ADSL."  ## ── No separator rule ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("hsides") |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     .separator = FALSE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Right-aligned footnotes ───────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     .align = "right"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [right] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Clear all footnotes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_footnotes("[a] Old footnote.") spec <- spec |> fr_footnotes()  # removes all footnotes

what: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: convenience wrapper calls fr_hlines() fr_vlines() one step. Use fr_grid() want set rule directions consistent styling (width, colour, linestyle). Prefer fr_grid() chaining fr_hlines() |> fr_vlines() : want uniform width/colour/linestyle directions. use slash shorthand concise one-liners: fr_grid("header/box"). Prefer separate fr_hlines() + fr_vlines() calls : Horizontal vertical rules need different widths, colours, styles. need fr_vlines(cols = ...) custom column positions.

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
code: fr_grid(   spec,   hpreset = "header",   vpreset = "box",   width = NULL,   color = NULL,   linestyle = NULL )

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). hpreset Horizontal rule preset passed fr_hlines(). Default "header" (single rule column header — standard TFL output). Alternatively, pass single slash-separated string combining presets: "header/box", "booktabs/", etc. / detected, left side used hpreset right side vpreset. vpreset Vertical rule preset passed fr_vlines(). Default "box" (outer border ). Ignored hpreset contains /. width Rule width applied horizontal vertical rules. Named shorthand ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick") numeric points. NULL uses preset's default. color Rule colour directions. NULL = black. linestyle Line style directions. NULL = "solid".

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: modified fr_spec horizontal vertical rules set.

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid

text: single owner header presentation settings: alignment, vertical alignment, N-count label formatting, bold, background/foreground colour, font size. defaults apply column header cells unless overridden per-column fr_col(header_align = ...) fr_style(region = "header", ...). fr_cols() owns column structure (labels, widths, body alignment, visibility); fr_header() owns header presentation. overlap — N-count formatting, header alignment, header valign live exclusively . N-count label formatting deferred — n format values stored spec resolved rendering. makes fr_header() fr_cols() order-independent (ggplot2 pattern). Calling fr_header() replaces previous header config (except spans, managed fr_spans()).

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
code: fr_header(   spec,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   n = NULL,   format = NULL,   n_subject = NULL,   n_data = NULL,   bold = NULL,   bg = NULL,   fg = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   repeat_on_page = NULL,   span_gap = NULL )

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). align Default horizontal alignment header cells. One "left", "center", "right", NULL (inherit column). Overridden per-column fr_col(header_align = ...). valign Default vertical alignment. One "top", "middle", "bottom" (default). Relevant header rows unequal height. n Subject counts N-count column header labels. Four forms: Named numeric vector — global N, page groups. Names must match data column names. Example: c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 44). Named list named numeric vectors — per-group N. List names must match page_by group values. element named numeric vector keyed column name. Example: list("Blood Pressure" = c(placebo = 42, zom_50mg = 40), "Heart Rate" = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 44)). Function — called per page group compute N dynamically. Must accept exactly two arguments return named numeric vector keyed column name: group_data data frame: subset n_data (spec$data n_data NULL) filtered current page_by group. example, page_by = "param" current page "Systolic BP (mmHg)", group_data contains rows PARAM == "Systolic BP (mmHg)". page_by, group_data full dataset. group_label character string: display label current page_by group (e.g. "Systolic BP (mmHg)"). NULL page_by set. Useful conditional counting logic varies group — example, excluding baseline visits certain parameters, using different subject-ID column per group. functions need group_data (subset already filtering). group_label available edge cases counting logic must differ group. arguments always passed tlframe, function must accept even ignores group_label. Example: function(d, gl) c(placebo = length(unique(d$USUBJID[d$TRT01A == "Placebo"]))). "auto" — auto-compute unique subjects per column per group. Requires n_subject (subject ID column name). Uses n_data provided, otherwise counts spec$data. display column name present source data, counts unique n_subject values column non-missing. Best suited wide-format source data column names match display table. long-format CDISC data (e.g., TRTA column), use function form instead. format glue-style format string N-count labels. Available tokens: {name} (column label) {n} (count). Default NULL (N-count formatting). Example: "{name}\n(N={n})". n_subject Character scalar. Column name containing subject identifiers (e.g. "USUBJID"). Required n = "auto". column must exist n_data (spec$data n_data NULL). n_data Optional data frame N-count computation. provided, "auto" function forms n operate dataset instead spec$data. Useful display table pre-summarized N-counts must come record-level source data (e.g. ADSL, ADVS). data frame must contain page_by column(s) per-group counts needed. bold Logical NULL. Whether header cells bold. Default NULL inherits built-default (FALSE). Set TRUE explicitly want bold headers. bg Background colour header cells: hex string ("#003366") CSS named colour ("steelblue", "lightgray", etc.). NULL inherits. fg Foreground (text) colour header cells: hex string CSS named colour. NULL inherits. font_size Font size points header cells. NULL inherits page font size. repeat_on_page Logical. Whether repeat column header every page. Default TRUE (standard regulatory tables). span_gap Logical NULL. Whether insert narrow gap columns adjacent spanning headers level. Gap columns create clean visual break span groups RTF PDF output, without relying border trimming. Default TRUE. Set FALSE disable gap insertion produce continuous span hlines.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). preset Named rule preset. One : "header" — single rule column header . Default common TFL outputs (ICH E3). top bottom border; footnotes follow last body row directly. "open" — rule header + rule header; bottom border. Use table bottom immediately followed footnotes. "hsides" — rule top (header) bottom (body) . "" — single rule column header . "" — single rule last body row . "box" — full outer border four sides. "booktabs" — thick top (1 pt) + thin mid (0.5 pt) + thick bottom (1 pt). standard PDF publication output. "void" — horizontal rules. Clears previously set rules. width Rule width. Named shorthand numeric points: "hairline" = 0.25 pt "thin" = 0.5 pt (default preset width) "medium" = 1.0 pt "thick" = 1.5 pt Numeric, e.g. width = 0.75 (positive, pt) supplied, overrides preset's default widths rules created call. NULL uses preset's default widths. color Rule colour: hex string ("#003366") CSS named colour ("navy"). NULL = black ("#000000"). linestyle Rule line style. One "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "dashdot", "double".

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: pharma TFL outputs use "header": single thin solid rule separating column headers body. top bottom border. Footnotes placed directly last body row short separator line drawn footnote block (controlled .separator argument fr_footnotes()). "booktabs" standard PDF/publication output matches LaTeX \toprule / \midrule / \bottomrule pattern.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: Combine fr_hlines() fr_vlines() independently — manages set rules. fr_hlines("void") clears horizontal rules; vertical rules fr_vlines() affected. width, color, linestyle apply uniformly rules created preset call. mixed widths (e.g. booktabs thick/thin), omit width let preset define widths.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
code: ## ── Common presets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Standard TFL output: single rule under column header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Open: header rules, no bottom border tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("open") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Booktabs: publication-quality thick/thin/thick tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("booktabs") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 3 hline(s)  # Top and bottom rules only tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("hsides") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Box: full outer border tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Remove all horizontal rules tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("void") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom width ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Hairline rules (0.25 pt) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", width = "hairline") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Thick rules (1.5 pt) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("hsides", width = "thick") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Exact width in points tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", width = 0.75) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Custom colour (hex or CSS named colour) ────────────────────────────────  # Hex colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("booktabs", color = "#003366") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 3 hline(s)  # CSS named colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", color = "steelblue") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Custom linestyle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Dashed rule below header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "dashed") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Double rule below header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "double") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Dash-dot (SAS ODS equivalent) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "dashdot") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Full pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Full Analysis Set") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: Sets page-level properties: paper size, orientation, margins, font, pagination controls. arguments explicitly supply changed — others retain current values. allows incremental updates anywhere pipeline.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
code: fr_page(   spec,   orientation = NULL,   paper = NULL,   margins = NULL,   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   orphan_min = NULL,   widow_min = NULL,   continuation = NULL,   col_gap = NULL,   tokens = NULL )

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). orientation "landscape" (default, 11 × 8.5 Letter) "portrait" (8.5 × 11 ). paper "letter" (default, 8.5 × 11 ), "a4" (210 × 297 mm), "legal" (8.5 × 14 ). margins Margin(s) inches. Accepts: Scalar: 1 — four sides equal. Length 2: c(vertical, horizontal) — top/bottom vs left/right. Length 4: c(top, right, bottom, left) — CSS order. Named list: list(top=1, bottom=1, left=0.75, right=0.75). font_family Font family name. Defaults OS monospace font ("Courier New" Windows/macOS, "Liberation Mono" Linux). Regulatory submissions typically use Courier New 9pt. font_size Font size points. Default 9. Typical pharma range: 7–10 pt. orphan_min Minimum body rows keep bottom page forcing page break. Default 3L. Set 1L disable. widow_min Minimum body rows carry top next page. Default 3L. Set 1L disable. continuation Character scalar appended column header block continuation pages, e.g. "(continued)". NULL (default) disables. col_gap Inter-column padding points (total gap adjacent columns). Default 4 (2 pt left + 2 pt right per cell). Increase 6 8 long text adjacent columns runs together. Set 0 zero padding (cells flush, legacy behaviour). Applied symmetrically half value side every cell RTF PDF output. tokens Named list custom {token} values use page headers/footers. E.g. list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", pop = "FAS"). Use tokens fr_pagehead() fr_pagefoot() strings.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
