title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: FDA/US submissions (eCTD): landscape Letter (8.5 × 11 ), Courier New 9 pt, 1 margins sides. common regulatory RTF standard package default. EMA/European submissions: A4 paper (210 × 297 mm) acceptable; landscape A4 common wide safety tables. Database cutoff date mandatory element every CSR table. standard practice define custom token:   Continuation label: multi-page tables must indicate continuation. Regulatory convention append "(continued)" column header block pages first:
code: fr_page(tokens = list(cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |> fr_pagehead(left  = "Study: {study}",             right = "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}") fr_page(continuation = "(continued)")

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: tokens clean way inject study-level metadata (study number, cutoff date, population) running headers without repeating value every fr_pagehead() call. orphan_min = 3 means: fewer 3 rows remain bottom page group_by group, move group next page.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
code: ## ── Standard pharma RTF setup ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(     orientation = "landscape",     paper       = "letter",     font_family = "Courier New",     font_size   = 9,     margins     = 1   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── A4 portrait for European submissions ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "portrait", paper = "a4", font_size = 9) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: portrait a4, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Narrow margins to fit wide table ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(margins = c(1, 0.5))   # top/bottom = 1in, left/right = 0.5in #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom tokens for running header ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", pop = "FAS")) |>   fr_pagehead(left  = "Study: {study}  Population: {pop}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Continuation label on multi-page tables ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Tight orphan/widow control ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orphan_min = 2L, widow_min = 2L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Wider inter-column padding ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(col_gap = 8)   # 4 pt each side (generous spacing) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New, col_gap=8pt #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Legal paper size (8.5 x 14 in) ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(paper = "legal", orientation = "landscape", margins = 1) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape legal, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: Sets running footer printed bottom every page. footer uses three-zone (left, center, right) system fr_pagehead(). Supports {token} placeholders, evaluated render time: {program} — R script filename (rstudioapi sys.call). {datetime} — run datetime DDMONYYYY HH:MM:SS format (e.g. "05MAR2026 14:24:25"). {thepage}, {total_pages}, custom tokens also supported. Calling fr_pagefoot() replaces previous footer.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
code: fr_pagefoot(   spec,   left = NULL,   center = NULL,   right = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   bold = FALSE )

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). left Character scalar NULL. Left zone text. center Character scalar NULL. Center zone text. right Character scalar NULL. Right zone text. three zones support token placeholders evaluated render time. font_size Font size points. NULL inherits page. bold Logical. Default FALSE.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: modified fr_spec. Footer stored spec$pagefoot.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: Standard pharma table shells specify following footer content every table page: Left zone: Program path filename. Use {program} token, resolves R script filename render time. Center zone: Confidentiality label needed. Right zone: Data source name, data extract date, run datetime. Full submission-standard footer:   {datetime} token resolves DDMONYYYY HH:MM:SS render time (e.g. "05MAR2026 14:24:25").
code: fr_page(tokens = list(datasource = "ADSL", extract = "01JAN2025")) |> fr_pagefoot(   left  = "{program}",   right = "Data Source: {datasource}  Extract: {extract}  Run: {datetime}" )

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
code: ## ── Program path + run datetime (most common footer pattern) ─────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Full submission-standard footer: program + data source + runtime ──────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(datasource = "ADSL", extract = "01JAN2025")) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "Data Source: {datasource}  Extract: {extract}  Run: {datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Program + confidentiality + datetime (3-zone) ─────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(     left   = "{program}",     center = "CONFIDENTIAL",     right  = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Minimal: page number only in footer ───────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Full page chrome: pagehead + pagefoot together ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: Sets running header printed top every page (every page first). header divided three independent zones: left, center, right. Supports {token} placeholders, evaluated render time: {thepage} — current page number. {total_pages} — total page count. Custom tokens defined via fr_page(tokens = list(...)). Calling fr_pagehead() replaces previous header.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
code: fr_pagehead(   spec,   left = NULL,   center = NULL,   right = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   bold = FALSE )

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). left Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Left zone text. Vectors collapsed newlines produce multi-line output. center Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Center zone text. right Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Right zone text. three zones support token placeholders (e.g. thepage) evaluated render time. font_size Font size header text points. NULL inherits page font size (fr_page(font_size = ...)). bold Logical. Whether header text bold. Default FALSE.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: modified fr_spec. Header stored spec$pagehead.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: ICH E3 specifies 3-part running header every table page: Left zone: Study/protocol identifier. Center zone: Compound name, document type, left blank. Right zone: "Page X Y". database cutoff date mandatory audit trail element. Standard table shells show right running header every page:   Implement :   Date format RTF submissions DDMONYYYY (e.g. "31DEC2024"), ISO 8601, match SAS ODS date conventions.
code: Study/Protocol: TFRM-2024-001          Database Cutoff Date: 31DEC2024 fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |> fr_pagehead(   left  = "Study/Protocol: {study}",   right = "Database Cutoff Date: {cutoff}" )

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: default, gap inserted page header. Add one fr_spacing():   can also set _tlframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(pagehead_after = 1L)   # one blank line spec |> fr_spacing(pagehead_after = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   pagehead_after: 1

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
code: ## ── Standard: Study/Protocol + Database Cutoff Date ──────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study  = "TFRM-2024-001",                         cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study/Protocol: {study}",     right = "Database Cutoff Date: {cutoff}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── 3-part header: study | CONFIDENTIAL | Page X of Y ────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left   = "Study/Protocol: {study}",     center = "CONFIDENTIAL",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Multi-token header with compound and population ───────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(     study    = "TFRM-2024-001",     compound = "Zomerane",     pop      = "FAS"   )) |>   fr_pagehead(     left   = "{study}  {compound}",     center = "Population: {pop}",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Smaller header font size ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagehead(     left      = "TFRM-2024-001",     right     = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}",     font_size = 7   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Combined pagehead + pagefoot in one pipeline ──────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom

what: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: Captures sequence fr_*() verb calls fr_recipe object can applied fr_spec. Recipes enable team-wide standardisation: define company style , reuse across hundreds tables. Apply recipe spec fr_apply(). Compose multiple recipes c(): c(recipe_a, recipe_b) creates combined recipe recipe_b overrides recipe_a verbs replace (verbs), appends verbs accumulate (fr_spans, fr_styles).

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
code: fr_recipe(...)

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: ... Unevaluated fr_*() verb calls. call must pipeline verb takes fr_spec first argument. spec argument omitted — injected recipe applied.

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: fr_recipe object (list captured call expressions).

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: fr_apply(): spec |> fr_apply(recipe) — pipe-friendly c(): c(recipe1, recipe2) — compose two recipes

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: Recipes store R language objects (calls), closures, survive saveRDS() / readRDS() roundtrips:
code: saveRDS(company_style, "company_style.rds") company_style <- readRDS("company_style.rds")

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
code: ## ── Recipe for page layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────  page_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}"),   fr_pagefoot(center = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") ) page_recipe #>  #> ── fr_recipe (3 verbs)  #> 1. fr_page(orientation="landscape", font_size=9) #> 2. fr_pagehead(left="{program}", right="{datetime}") #> 3. fr_pagefoot(center="Page {thepage} of {total_pages}")  ## ── Recipe for styling ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  style_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") ) style_recipe #>  #> ── fr_recipe (2 verbs)  #> 1. fr_hlines("header") #> 2. fr_header(bold=TRUE, align="center")  ## ── Apply a recipe then add per-table customisation ───────────────────────  company_style <- c(page_recipe, style_recipe)  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(company_style) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") spec #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1" #> 2. [center] "Demographics" #> Header: bold, valign=bottom, align=center #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Compose: base + override for AE tables ────────────────────────────────  ae_style <- c(company_style, fr_recipe(   fr_hlines("box"),   fr_page(font_size = 8) )) ae_spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(ae_style) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE Summary by SOC")

what: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: Registers new output format backend fr_render() can produce output format. Third-party packages can use add HTML, DOCX, formats without modifying tlframe source.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
code: fr_register_backend(format, extensions, render_fn, description = "")

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: format Character scalar. Format identifier (e.g., "html"). extensions Character vector. File extensions map format (e.g., c("html", "htm")). render_fn Function. render function signature function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path). description Character scalar. Human-readable description.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
code: # Register a stub HTML backend fr_register_backend(   format = "html",   extensions = c("html", "htm"),   render_fn = function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {     writeLines("<html><body>stub table</body></html>", path)   },   description = "Stub HTML tables" )  # Verify it appears in the backend list fr_backends() #>   format extensions               description #> 1    rtf   rtf, doc          Rich Text Format #> 2  latex        tex LaTeX source (tabularray) #> 3    pdf        pdf           PDF via XeLaTeX #> 4    csv        csv    CSV export (data only) #> 5   html  html, htm          Stub HTML tables  # Use the custom backend out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demo.html") tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_render(out) readLines(out) #> [1] "<html><body>stub table</body></html>" unlink(out)

what: Render a Table to File — fr_render
title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: terminal verb every tlframe pipeline. Takes fully configured fr_spec object writes rendered table file. Supports RTF, LaTeX, PDF output. Row pagination within RTF output delegated Word/LibreOffice rendering engine via native RTF properties (\trhdr repeating headers). LaTeX backend uses tabularray's longtblr environment rowhead repeating headers. R handles page_by group splitting (section/page breaks groups) col_split (column panel calculation wide tables).

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
code: fr_render(spec, path, format = NULL, ...)

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(), configured fr_*() pipeline verbs. path Character scalar. Output file path. file extension determines format unless format specified: .rtf — Rich Text Format. .tex — LaTeX source (tabularray/XeLaTeX). .pdf — PDF via XeLaTeX compilation (requires XeLaTeX PATH tinytex). format Character scalar NULL. Output format: "rtf", "latex", "pdf". NULL (default), detected file extension path. ... Reserved future backend-specific options.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: Invisibly returns path (output file path).

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: fr_rows(page_by = "col_name") set, data split unique values column. group rendered separate section table, separated \sect (section break) RTF. page-column value printed bold label group's table.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: fr_cols(.split = TRUE) set total column width exceeds printable page area, columns automatically split panels. Stub columns (set via fr_col(stub = TRUE)) repeated every panel. panel rendered separate section.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
code: # Render to a temporary RTF file out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demog.rtf") tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Full pipeline with titles, footnotes, and page chrome out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demog_full.rtf") tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Summary of Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL", "Program: t_demog.R") |>   fr_cols(.width = "auto") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape") |>   fr_pagehead(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_pagefoot(center = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Multiple format rendering in sequence (RTF then LaTeX source) rtf_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_soc.rtf") tex_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_soc.tex") spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape") fr_render(spec, rtf_out) fr_render(spec, tex_out) unlink(c(rtf_out, tex_out))  # Listing rendering — individual AE records out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_listing.rtf") adae |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.2),     AEDECOD = fr_col("Preferred Term"),     AESEV   = fr_col("Severity", width = 1.0)   ) |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT"),           repeat_cols = "USUBJID") |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7", "Adverse Events") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADAE") |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Figure rendering (requires ggplot2) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(adsl, ggplot2::aes(x = AGE)) +     ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +     ggplot2::labs(title = NULL, x = "Age (years)", y = "Count")   out <- file.path(tempdir(), "age_dist.rtf")   p |>     fr_figure() |>     fr_titles("Figure 14.1.1", "Distribution of Age") |>     fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") |>     fr_page(orientation = "landscape") |>     fr_render(out)   unlink(out) }

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: rows Integer vector body row positions, "", NULL (body rows). Row indices 1-based. Multiple indices supported: rows = c(1L, 3L, 5L). bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. fg Foreground (text) colour, NULL. bg Background (fill) colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. valign Vertical alignment: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. height Row height inches, NULL (auto).

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: fr_cell_style object type = "row" use fr_styles().

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: multiple styles target cell, narrower scopes win:   Within scope level, later styles (later fr_styles() call later fr_styles() calls) override earlier ones. fr_row_style() overrides fr_col_style() property row styles narrower column styles.
code: fr_col_style  <  fr_row_style  <  fr_style (cell)

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: Use fr_row_style(rows = "", bg = "#F5F5F5") subtle background body rows (zebra striping requires alternating calls: rows odd/even index). Use fr_rows_matches() instead hard-coded row indices content-based row targeting (e.g. bold every "Total" row).

what: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: Controls data rows grouped, paginated, indented, spaced. Calling fr_rows() replaces previous row configuration. tlframe uses two-level greedy pagination algorithm (identical RTF PDF output): page_by forces page break whenever named column(s) change value. group_by keeps rows value named column(s) page via \trkeep (RTF). group large single page, breaks normally "(continued)" header row repeats top next page.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
code: fr_rows(   spec,   page_by = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   indent_by = NULL,   blank_after = NULL,   page_by_bold = FALSE,   page_by_align = "left",   sort_by = NULL,   repeat_cols = NULL,   wrap = FALSE )

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). page_by Character vector column name(s). new page begins whenever columns change value. column value rendered group label column headers, column automatically hidden table body render time. Use multi-SOC tables break System Organ Class. group_by Character vector column name(s). Rows sharing value visually grouped (indented detail rows, blank-spacing) kept together page possible. group spans pages, "(continued)" header row repeats top next page. indent_by Character vector column name(s). Rows indented relative parent group level, creating hierarchy. Typically used PT rows SOC. blank_after Character vector column name(s). blank row inserted group boundary columns. page_by_bold Logical. Whether page-label rendered bold. Default FALSE. Set TRUE make label stand column headers. page_by_align Horizontal alignment page-label. One "left" (default), "center", "right", "decimal". sort_by Character vector column name(s). Sorts data columns rendering. listings, controls display order (e.g., sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT")). Sorting applied finalize_spec(). repeat_cols Character vector column name(s). Suppresses repeated consecutive values columns — first occurrence run displayed. Standard listings subject ID appears per block. Suppression applied finalize_spec(). wrap Logical. TRUE, enables text wrapping body cells. Default FALSE. listings long text fields (e.g., verbatim terms, medical history), wrapping prevents cell content overflowing.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: modified fr_spec. Row config stored spec$body.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: Standard pharma AE tables (MedDRA SOC/PT hierarchy) follow row conventions: Sort order: SOC rows sorted descending incidence across arms; within SOC, PT rows sorted descending incidence (alphabetical tiebreaker). standard regulatory sort order. "TOTAL SUBJECTS EVENT" always first summary row, first SOC. Pre-compute data frame. Subject counting: subject counted per SOC per PT, even multiple events. Document fr_footnotes(). Page breaks SOC: System Organ Class starts new page (page_by = "soc"). PT rows stay SOC header via group_by. Indentation: PT rows indented SOC header (indent_by = "pt_label"). Indent: 0.1667 (2 spaces / 240 twips).

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: demographics continuous endpoint tables, standard summary statistic row order : n, Mean, SD (SE), Median, Q1/Q3, Min/Max Pre-sort long-form data match order. Use blank_after insert visual separation characteristic blocks (e.g. Age Sex sections).

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: pagination engine respects orphan_min widow_min settings fr_page(). Orphan control prevents lone row stranded bottom page; widow control prevents lone row starting new page. apply within group_by groups: placing group leave fewer orphan_min rows page break, group moved next page instead.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: page_by columns automatically hidden render time — values appear group labels section header, need appear table body. Override fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = TRUE)) needed (set fr_rows()). group_by, indent_by, etc. columns structural — hide via fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) appear. Use group_by (page_by) want rows travel together necessarily start new page. blank_after simplest way add visual group separation without bold headers.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
code: ## ── AE table paginated by System Organ Class ─────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc  ## ── Demographics table: blank row after each group ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(blank_after = "group") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Indented hierarchy (SOC bold header + indented PT rows) ──────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = "pt"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=soc, indent_by=pt  ## ── Combined: page_by + group_by + indent ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(soc = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     page_by   = "soc",     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = "pt"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Columns (5 visible of 7): #> pt "pt" 4.05in left #> placebo "placebo" 0.82in left #> zom_50mg "zom_50mg" 0.82in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.90in left #> total "total" 0.90in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc, group_by=soc, indent_by=pt  ## ── sort_by: order a listing by subject and start date ────────────────  adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "ASTDT", "AESEV")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT")) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 20 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,ASTDT  ## ── repeat_cols: suppress repeated subject IDs in a listing ───────────  adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AESEV")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     sort_by     = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS"),     repeat_cols = "USUBJID"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 20 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,AEBODSYS  ## ── wrap = TRUE: enable text wrapping for long verbatim terms ─────────  adae[1:10, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AEOUT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(wrap = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 10 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: wrap  ## ── Combined: sort_by + repeat_cols on adverse event listing ──────────  adae[1:30, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "ASTDT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     sort_by     = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD"),     repeat_cols = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS"),     wrap        = TRUE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 30 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,AEBODSYS,AEDECOD, wrap

what: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: Creates row selector object use rows argument fr_row_style() fr_style(). style-application time (inside fr_styles()), selector evaluated table data frame produce matching row positions. avoids hard-coding integer row numbers styling rows content — example, bolding every "Total" row colouring every p-value row red. selector data-driven: rows reordered filtered, styles automatically track correct rows.

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
code: fr_rows_matches(col, value = NULL, pattern = NULL, ignore.case = FALSE)

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: col Character scalar. Name data column match . Must exist data frame passed fr_table(). value Scalar. Exact value match (using ==). Mutually exclusive pattern. Supports atomic type (character, numeric, logical). pattern Character scalar. regular expression passed grep() (Perl-compatible). Mutually exclusive value. regex matched character representation column values. ignore.case Logical. Whether pattern matching case-insensitive. Default FALSE. Ignored value used.

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: fr_rows_selector object use fr_row_style() fr_style().

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
code: ## ── Bold every "Total" row ────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", "Total"), bold = TRUE)   ) #> ℹ `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: value Total. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Red text for p-value rows (regex) ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "^p[- .]?value",                               ignore.case = TRUE),       fg = "#CC0000", italic = TRUE     )   ) #> ℹ `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: pattern ^p[- .]?value. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Combine with cell styles ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     # Shade every SOC header row     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", "soc"), bg = "#F0F0F0", bold = TRUE),     # Red text for all PT rows with a p-value pattern     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", "pt"), fg = "#333333")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Alternation pattern: match "Total" or "Subtotal" ────────────────────  fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "Total|Subtotal") #> $col #> [1] "characteristic" #>  #> $value #> NULL #>  #> $pattern #> [1] "Total|Subtotal" #>  #> $ignore.case #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_rows_selector"  ## ── Character class pattern: rows starting with uppercase letter ───────  fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "^[A-Z]") #> $col #> [1] "characteristic" #>  #> $value #> NULL #>  #> $pattern #> [1] "^[A-Z]" #>  #> $ignore.case #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_rows_selector"  ## ── Combined with fr_style in a pipeline ──────────────────────────────────  tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("category", pattern = "Completed|Discontinued"),       bold = TRUE, bg = "#F0F0F0"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 8 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override

what: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: Sets number blank lines inserted table sections. gaps provide visual separation running header, titles, group label, column headers, body rows, footnotes, running footer. five controllable junctions table anatomy:   Calling fr_spacing() replaces specified values. Unspecified arguments keep current value.
code: [Page Header]   --- pagehead_after --- Titles   --- titles_after --- Page-by Label   --- page_by_after --- Column Headers / Body Rows   --- footnotes_before --- Footnotes   --- pagefoot_before --- [Page Footer]

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
code: fr_spacing(   spec,   titles_after = NULL,   footnotes_before = NULL,   pagehead_after = NULL,   pagefoot_before = NULL,   page_by_after = NULL )

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). titles_after Integer. Blank lines last title column header rule (page-label page_by set). Default 1L. Set 0L gap. footnotes_before Integer. Blank lines last body row (bottom rule) first footnote. Default 1L. Set 0L gap. pagehead_after Integer. Blank lines running page header first title. Default 0L (gap). Set 1L one blank line. takes effect page header set via fr_pagehead(). pagefoot_before Integer. Blank lines last footnote running page footer. Default 0L. Set 1L add breathing room footer. takes effect page footer set via fr_pagefoot(). page_by_after Integer. Blank lines page-label column header row. Default 1L. takes effect page_by set via fr_rows(). Set 0L gap.

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: modified fr_spec. Spacing stored spec$spacing.

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: Standard pharma submission tables (ICH E3, FDA/EMA) typically use one blank line titles footnotes. package defaults reflect minimal layout: titles_after = 1L — one blank line titles footnotes_before = 1L — one blank line footnotes pagehead_after = 0L — gap (user decides) pagefoot_before = 0L — gap (user decides) page_by_after = 1L — one blank line page-label

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: five spacing values can set _tlframe.yml:
code: spacing:   titles_after: 1   footnotes_before: 1   pagehead_after: 0   pagefoot_before: 0   page_by_after: 1

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
code: ## ── Default spacing (1 blank line at each gap) ───────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Safety Population") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Safety Population" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── No gap after titles (compact layout) ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spacing(titles_after = 0L) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Two blank lines before footnotes ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 2L) |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Space after group label (page_by) ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") |>   fr_spacing(page_by_after = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc  ## ── Space before page footer ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_spacing(pagefoot_before = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── All five spacing parameters in one call ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events by SOC") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] MedDRA v26.1.") |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") |>   fr_pagehead(left = "TFRM-2024-001") |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}") |>   fr_spacing(     titles_after     = 1L,     footnotes_before = 1L,     pagehead_after   = 1L,     pagefoot_before  = 1L,     page_by_after    = 1L   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events by SOC" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] MedDRA v26.1."  ## ── Tight layout: zero spacing everywhere ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] N = number of subjects.") |>   fr_spacing(     titles_after     = 0L,     footnotes_before = 0L,     pagehead_after   = 0L,     pagefoot_before  = 0L,     page_by_after    = 0L   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] N = number of subjects."

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Adds one spanning headers — labels sit group columns additional header row. named argument creates one span: argument name span label; value character vector column names covered span. Calling fr_spans() appends spans already defined. Call multiple times different .level values build multi-level spanning headers.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
code: fr_spans(spec, ..., .level = 1L, .hline = TRUE)

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... Named column selections. name span label; value either character vector column names tidyselect expression: Character vector: "Treatment" = c("col1", "col2") Tidyselect: "Treatment" = starts_with("zom_") Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), . .level Integer. Vertical level span row (1L = immediately column header row; higher numbers ). Default 1L. .hline Logical. Whether draw thin horizontal line spanned columns. Default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress border.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: modified fr_spec. Spans appended spec$header$spans.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Standard pharma house styles recommend: Use spanning headers necessary. Avoid simple two-arm tables column label already identifies treatment group. used, span label typically carries treatment arm name timepoint period, individual column labels carry dose level N (e.g. "10 mg\n(N=45)"). Common patterns: Single-level span grouping dose arms compound name: "Zomerane" = c("low_dose", "high_dose") Two-level span: dose arms level 1, compound level 2. Timepoint spans: "Week 12" = c("w12_n", "w12_pct").

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Build inner outer (lowest level first):   Level 1 spans sit directly column labels; level 2 spans sit level 1.
code: spec |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n", "high_n"), .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("Active Treatment" = c("low_n", "high_n"), .level = 2L)

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Span labels support {fr_*()} inline markup (e.g. "{fr_bold('Treatment')}"). column can appear one span per level. Overlapping spans level produce undefined layout behaviour — avoid . Columns covered span given level display empty cell span row. .hline border helps visually separate span column labels . Set .hline = FALSE spanning purely decorative (e.g. grouping timepoints without visible divider). Span alignment inherits fr_header() align set, otherwise defaults center.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
code: ## ── Single-level treatment group span ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic",    width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",             width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg",            width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",            width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",              width = 1.5, align = "right")   ) |>   fr_header(     n = c(zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, placebo = 45, total = 135),     format = "{name}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 1.50in right #> zom_50mg "50 mg" 1.50in right #> zom_100mg "100 mg" 1.50in right #> total "Total" 1.50in right #> group "group" 0.90in left #> Header: valign=bottom, N-counts #> Spans: 1  ## ── Two-level spanning header ─────────────────────────────────────────────  # Build a small example with dose-level columns dose_data <- data.frame(   param = "Weight", low_n = "43", low_pct = "31.9%",   high_n = "47", high_pct = "34.8%" ) dose_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans("10 mg"    = c("low_n",  "low_pct"),  .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("25 mg"    = c("high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n",  "low_pct",                            "high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 2L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 1 row x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 3  ## ── Markup in span label ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "{fr_bold('Zomerane')}" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Span without bottom border (.hline = FALSE) ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     .hline = FALSE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Single-column span (acts as a group label above one column) ────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     "Reference" = "placebo"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 2  ## ── Tidyselect: span columns by pattern ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = starts_with("zom_")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Span + fr_header bold interaction ──────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE) |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: bold, valign=bottom #> Spans: 1

what: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: Creates cell-level style object use fr_styles(). Targets individual cells row column position (region keyword). properties explicitly set override base style; NULL means "inherit base style". row-level column-level styling, prefer fr_row_style() fr_col_style() simpler interface uniform row/column formatting.

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
code: fr_style(   region = "body",   rows = NULL,   cols = NULL,   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   fg = NULL,   bg = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   indent = NULL,   colspan = NULL,   rowspan = NULL )

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: region Region target. One : "body" (default) — data body rows. "header" — column header row(s) spanner row(s). "stub" — first (row-label) column across body rows. multiple stub columns, use region = "body" cols = c("lab_name", "visit") instead. rows Integer vector row positions, "" (rows region), NULL (rows). Row indices 1-based relative region. Multiple indices supported: rows = c(1L, 3L, 5L). cols Column selection. Accepts : character vector column names: cols = c("zom_50mg", "placebo") tidyselect expression: cols = starts_with("zom_") NULL (default) — targets columns. Tidyselect expressions captured unevaluated resolved style applied via fr_styles(). means can create style objects tidyselect expressions data available. Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), tidyselect::everything(), tidyselect::(), . bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. fg Foreground (text) colour: hex string ("#003366") 148 CSS named colours ("navy", "steelblue", "tomato", etc.). NULL inherit. bg Background (fill) colour: hex string CSS named colour. NULL inherit. font_size Font size points, NULL inherit. align Horizontal alignment override: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. Controls text flows within cell (left--right positioning). RTF maps \ql, \qc, \qr; LaTeX l, c, r column types. See Alignment model . valign Vertical alignment override: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. Controls content sits within cell row taller content (e.g., due multi-line labels adjacent cells). RTF maps \clvertalt, \clvertalc, \clvertalb; LaTeX p (top), m (middle), b (bottom). See Alignment model . indent Indentation inches, NULL. colspan Integer. Number columns cell spans (horizontal merge). NULL = span. rowspan Integer. Number rows cell spans (vertical merge). NULL = span.

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: fr_cell_style object use fr_styles().

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: tlframe separates alignment two orthogonal axes, following model RTF LaTeX/tabularray: align (horizontal) paragraph-level property — controls line text positioned within cell width. cells need . valign (vertical) cell-level property — controls content block sits cell taller needed. matters rows unequal content height (e.g., single-line label next multi-line label). single-line body rows, valign visible effect. combine freely: cell can align = "right", valign = "bottom" (text right-aligned, sitting bottom tall row).

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style

what: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: Diagnostic tool reports cell styles affect specific cell table body, order applied, final resolved properties. Use cell look expected rendered output — shows exactly style layers active combine.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
code: fr_style_explain(spec, row, col)

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). row Integer scalar. Body row index (1-based). col Character scalar integer. Column name column index.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: Invisibly returns list : $final — named list resolved properties (bold, italic, fg, bg, align, valign, indent, font_size). $layers — ordered list matching styles index spec$cell_styles, type, overridden properties. Prints human-readable summary console showing: cell content value. matching style layer index, type (col/row/cell), properties sets. final resolved properties layers applied.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: [1], [2] indices refer position spec$cell_styles. Properties later layers override earlier ones. example, column style sets background; row style adds bold.
code: -- Style explain: row 1, col "total" -- Content: "135" 2 matching styles:   [1] col: bg="#EBF5FB"   [2] row: bold=TRUE  Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, fg=#000000, bg=#EBF5FB, ...

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB"),     fr_row_style(rows = 1L, bold = TRUE)   )  # See which styles affect row 1, column "total" fr_style_explain(spec, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 2 matching styles: #> [1] col: bg="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, fg=#000000, bg=#EBF5FB, align=left, valign=top, #> indent=0  # Programmatic access to the resolved properties result <- fr_style_explain(spec, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 2 matching styles: #> [1] col: bg="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, fg=#000000, bg=#EBF5FB, align=left, valign=top, #> indent=0 result$final$bold   # TRUE (from row style) #> [1] TRUE result$final$bg     # "#EBF5FB" (from col style) #> [1] "#EBF5FB"  ## ── Multiple overlapping styles: see precedence in action ─────────────────  spec2 <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB"),     fr_row_style(rows = 1L, bg = "#FFF3CD", bold = TRUE),     fr_style(region = "body", rows = 1L, cols = "total",              fg = "#CC0000", italic = TRUE)   )  # Cell (1, "total") has three overlapping layers — cell wins for fg/italic, # row wins for bg/bold (narrower scope), col style is overridden: fr_style_explain(spec2, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 3 matching styles: #> [1] col: bg="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE, bg="#FFF3CD" #> [3] cell: italic=TRUE, fg="#CC0000" #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=TRUE, fg=#CC0000, bg=#FFF3CD, align=left, valign=top, #> indent=0  ## For header-region styles, # review spec3$cell_styles directly: str(spec3$cell_styles) #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 17 #>   ..$ type     : chr "cell" #>   ..$ region   : chr "header" #>   ..$ rows     : NULL #>   ..$ cols     : NULL #>   ..$ bold     : logi TRUE #>   ..$ italic   : NULL #>   ..$ underline: NULL #>   ..$ fg       : NULL #>   ..$ bg       : chr "#E0E0E0" #>   ..$ font     : NULL #>   ..$ font_size: NULL #>   ..$ align    : NULL #>   ..$ valign   : NULL #>   ..$ indent   : NULL #>   ..$ colspan  : NULL #>   ..$ rowspan  : NULL #>   ..$ height   : NULL #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "fr_cell_style" #>  $ :List of 17 #>   ..$ type     : chr "cell" #>   ..$ region   : chr "header" #>   ..$ rows     : NULL #>   ..$ cols     : chr "total" #>   ..$ bold     : NULL #>   ..$ italic   : NULL #>   ..$ underline: NULL #>   ..$ fg       : NULL #>   ..$ bg       : chr "#D0E4FF" #>   ..$ font     : NULL #>   ..$ font_size: NULL #>   ..$ align    : NULL #>   ..$ valign   : NULL #>   ..$ indent   : NULL #>   ..$ colspan  : NULL #>   ..$ rowspan  : NULL #>   ..$ height   : NULL #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "fr_cell_style"

what: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: Creates data-driven style evaluated cell values application time (inside fr_styles()). Rows matching condition receive specified style properties. avoids hard-coding row indices styling based content.

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
code: fr_style_if(   condition,   cols = NULL,   apply_to = "cell",   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   fg = NULL,   bg = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL )

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: condition one-sided formula function returns logical vector. formula uses .x pronoun column values (converted via rlang::as_function()): ~ .x == "Total" — match exact text ~ .numeric(.x) < 0.05 — numeric comparison ~ grepl("^N=", .x) — pattern matching cols = NULL, .x receives seq_len(nrow(data)) (row indices), useful zebra striping: ~ (.x %% 2) == 0. cols Column selection condition. Accepts : character vector column names: cols = c("zom_50mg", "placebo") tidyselect expression: cols = starts_with("zom_") NULL (default) — row-index-based conditions (e.g., zebra striping). condition applied values selected column independently. Tidyselect expressions resolved style applied via fr_styles(). apply_to Character scalar. Controls scope matched rows: "cell" (default): style cells condition TRUE. "row": style columns rows condition TRUE. bold, italic, underline Logical NULL leave unchanged. fg Foreground (text) colour: hex string CSS named colour, NULL. bg Background (fill) colour: hex string CSS named colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment ("left", "center", "right", "decimal"), NULL.

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: fr_conditional_style object use fr_styles().

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
code: ## ── Bold "Total" rows ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "characteristic",       condition = ~ .x == "Total",       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Zebra striping ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       condition = ~ (.x %% 2) == 0,       bg = "#F5F5F5",       apply_to = "row"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Numeric condition: highlight small p-values ──────────────────────────  # Create a table with a p-value column pval_data <- data.frame(   characteristic = c("Age", "Sex", "Weight"),   treatment = c("50 (23.5)", "30 (14.1)", "45 (21.1)"),   placebo   = c("55 (25.8)", "28 (13.1)", "52 (24.4)"),   pvalue    = c("0.042", "0.310", "0.003"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  pval_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "pvalue",       condition = ~ as.numeric(.x) < 0.05,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, fg = "#CC0000"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 3 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Pattern matching with grepl ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "soc",       condition = ~ grepl("SKIN|GASTROINTESTINAL", .x, ignore.case = TRUE),       apply_to = "row",       bg = "#FFF3CD"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── apply_to = "cell": colour only the matching cell ───────────────────────  tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),       condition = ~ grepl("0", .x),       apply_to = "cell",       fg = "#999999", italic = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 8 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Function form (not formula) for the condition ──────────────────────────  is_total <- function(x) x == "Total"  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "characteristic",       condition = is_total,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, bg = "#E8E8E8"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Multiple cols: evaluate condition on several columns ───────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg", "total"),       condition = ~ grepl("^0", .x),       apply_to = "cell",       fg = "#999999"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides

what: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: Appends one style override objects — created fr_style(), fr_row_style(), fr_col_style() — table specification. Multiple calls fr_styles() accumulate: styles applied order, later styles overriding earlier ones target cells.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
code: fr_styles(spec, ...)

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One style objects created fr_style(), fr_row_style(), fr_col_style().

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: modified fr_spec. Styles appended spec$cell_styles.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: Styles applied order passed. precedence (lowest highest) : Column styles (fr_col_style) — widest scope, applied first. Row styles (fr_row_style) — override column styles. Cell styles (fr_style) — narrowest scope, applied last. Within type, later styles override earlier ones cell properties. means can set broad default selectively override specific cells.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: idiomatic pass styles single fr_styles() call, ordering broad specific. fr_styles() appends — call fr_table() start fresh discard accumulated styles. number styles negligible performance impact — render engine applies single pass cell grid.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
code: ## ── Create reusable style objects, then apply ───────────────────────────── header_style <- fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, bg = "lavender") total_col    <- fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "aliceblue") bold_totals  <- fr_row_style(   rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", "Total"),   bold = TRUE )  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(header_style, total_col, bold_totals) #> ℹ `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: value Total. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Bold header + highlighted Total column ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE),     fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Zebra striping (alternating row shading) ─────────────────────────────  n <- nrow(tbl_demog) odd_rows  <- seq(1, n, by = 2) even_rows <- seq(2, n, by = 2)  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = odd_rows,  bg = "#FFFFFF"),     fr_row_style(rows = even_rows, bg = "#F5F5F5")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Multi-layer: col → row → cell ────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     # Broad: light background for all data columns     fr_col_style(cols = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg", "placebo"), bg = "#FAFAFA"),     # Mid: bold entire header     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE),     # Narrow: red text for high-risk row     fr_style(region = "body", rows = 3L, fg = "#CC0000", bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Stub column bold + header background ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, bg = "#E8E8E8"),     fr_style(region = "stub",   bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Accumulating styles across multiple calls ─────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE))  # Later: add Total column highlight (accumulates, does not replace) spec <- spec |>   fr_styles(fr_col_style(cols = "total", bg = "#EBF5FB"))

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: Renders content subscript final output. Use inside glue-string expressions titles, footnotes, column labels, cell values. Common clinical statistics chemical formulas (H₂O), statistical notation (beta-1), indexed variables (x_i).
