title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string, produces sentinel token resolved render time.

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: RTF output, subscript uses \sub (half font size, lowered). future LaTeX output, maps \textsubscript{}. subscript text renders roughly half surrounding font size.

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
code: # Chemical formula: H₂O fr_col("H{fr_sub(2)}O", width = 1.0) #> <fr_col> "H{fr_sub(2)}O" [1.00in, left]  # Statistical subscript: x_i fr_col("x{fr_sub('i')}", width = 1.0) #> <fr_col> "x{fr_sub('i')}" [1.00in, left]  # In a footnote: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_sub('n')} = number of subjects with data") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "n = number of subjects with data"

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: Renders content superscript final output. Use inside glue-string expressions titles, footnotes, column labels, cell values. render time backend converts markup appropriate format (RTF control words LaTeX \textsuperscript{}). common use regulatory tables footnote markers: "[]{fr_super('')}" cell value, paired "[] Percentages based N." fr_footnotes().

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
code: fr_super(x)

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: x Character numeric. Content render superscript. Coerced character internally.

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string (e.g. "{fr_super(1)}"), produces sentinel token (\x01SUPER:1\x02) resolved render time.

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
code: # Footnote marker fr_super("a") #> <fr_markup> ^{a}  # Unit with exponent: kg/m² fr_col("BMI kg/m{fr_super(2)}", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "BMI kg/m{fr_super(2)}" [1.50in, left]  # Standalone usage (inspect the sentinel) format(fr_super(1)) #> [1] "\001SUPER:1\002"  # In a pipeline: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() n <- 1 spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_super(n)} Pearson chi-square test") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "1 Pearson chi-square test"

what: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_table() entry point every tlframe pipeline. wraps presentation-ready data frame fr_spec object, configure fr_*() verbs finally render fr_render(). data frame already contain rows columns want display — tlframe summarise reshape data. Use packages gt, Tplyr, rtables, tidytlg, tfrmt summarise data, hand summary data frame fr_table().

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code: fr_table(data)

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: data data frame (tibble). Must already presentation-ready: row maps one table row, column one table column. summarisation performed tlframe.

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_spec object. Pass fr_*() verb via |>.

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code: data |>   fr_table()                        # start   |> fr_titles(...)                 # titles above the table   |> fr_footnotes(...)              # footnotes below   |> fr_cols(col = fr_col(...))     # column widths / labels   |> fr_header(n = ..., format = .) # N-count labels / header styling   |> fr_spans(...)                  # spanning headers   |> fr_rows(page_by = ...)         # pagination / grouping   |> fr_page(orientation = ...)     # page layout   |> fr_pagehead(left = ...)        # running header   |> fr_pagefoot(right = ...)       # running footer   |> fr_hlines("header")         # horizontal rules   |> fr_vlines("box")               # vertical rules   |> fr_styles(fr_row_style(...))   # cell / row / column styling   |> fr_render("output.rtf")        # render to file

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_*() verb can called multiple times; replace (append) previous setting. Exceptions: fr_spans() fr_styles() append repeated calls. Column order rendered table matches column order data. Reorder columns data calling fr_table() needed. Invisible columns (e.g. grouping keys) can hidden via fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) rather dropping data.

title: Start a tlframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code:
## ── Minimal: one-step pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |> fr_table() #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Typical regulatory pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on the number of subjects in each arm.") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on the number of subjects in each arm."  ## ── AE by SOC/PT with pagination ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",             "Safety Analysis Set") |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc", group_by = "soc") |>   fr_hlines("header") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Pre..." #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc, group_by=soc #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Store spec, reuse, branch ────────────────────────────────────────────  base_spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9)  # Branch A: portrait with smaller font spec_a <- base_spec |> fr_page(orientation = "portrait", font_size = 8)  # Branch B: add titles and render spec_b <- base_spec |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") |>   fr_hlines("booktabs")  ## ── Complete 10-verb pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────────── # Demonstrates every major pipeline verb in a single specification.  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on subjects in each arm.",     "Source: ADSL"   ) |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     total          = fr_col("Total
(N=135)", width = 1.5),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, bg = "#D9E2F3") |>   fr_spans("Treatment Arm" = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", indent_by = "group") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("inner") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE)   ) |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9) |>   fr_pagehead(left = "Study TFRM-2024-001", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_spacing(titles_after = 1, footnotes_before = 1) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo (N=45)" 1.50in left #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50mg (N=45)" 1.50in left #> zom_100mg "Zomerane 100mg (N..." 1.50in left #> total "Total (N=135)" 1.50in left #> Header: bold, valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=group, indent_by=group #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Spans: 1 #> Styles: 1 override #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on subjects in each arm." #> 2. [left] "Source: ADSL"  ## ── fr_table() vs fr_listing() on the same data ────────────────────────── # fr_table() uses auto-detected defaults; fr_listing() sets listing defaults.  ae_subset <- adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "ASTDT")]  # Table view: auto-detected alignment, 9pt, no split spec_tbl <- ae_subset |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table View of AE Records") spec_tbl$page$font_size    # 9 (default) #> [1] 9  # Listing view: left-aligned, 8pt, split = TRUE, wrap = TRUE spec_lst <- ae_subset |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_titles("Listing View of AE Records") spec_lst$page$font_size          # 8 (listing default) #> [1] 8 spec_lst$columns_meta$split      # TRUE (listing default) #> [1] TRUE  ## ── Hidden column used for grouping ────────────────────────────────────── # The "group" column drives indent_by but is not displayed.  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_rows(indent_by = "group") |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics (grouped, hidden key)") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics (grouped, hidden key)" #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> characteristic "characteristic" 2.70in left #> placebo "placebo" 0.97in left #> zom_50mg "zom_50mg" 0.97in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.97in left #> total "total" 0.97in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: indent_by=group

what: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: fr_theme() sets study-level defaults automatically applied every subsequent fr_table() call. Define font size, page layout, tokens, running header/footer, line rules top program; tables inherit settings without repeating .

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
code: fr_theme(   orientation = NULL,   paper = NULL,   margins = NULL,   col_gap = NULL,   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   split = NULL,   stub = NULL,   pagehead = NULL,   pagefoot = NULL,   tokens = NULL,   hlines = NULL,   vlines = NULL,   spacing = NULL,   header = NULL,   footnote_separator = NULL )

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: orientation "landscape" "portrait". NULL leaves unchanged. paper "letter", "a4", "legal". NULL leaves unchanged. margins Margin(s) inches (formats fr_page()). NULL leaves unchanged. col_gap Inter-column padding points (integer). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_page() details. font_family Font family name. NULL leaves unchanged. font_size Font size points. NULL leaves unchanged. split Logical. TRUE enable column splitting wide tables, FALSE disable. NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_cols() .split parameter details. stub Character vector column names mark stub columns (repeated every panel column splitting). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_col() stub parameter details. pagehead Named list left, center, right, font_size, bold elements — fr_pagehead() arguments. NULL leaves unchanged. pagefoot Named list left, center, right, font_size, bold elements — fr_pagefoot() arguments. NULL leaves unchanged. tokens Named list {token} values. NULL leaves unchanged. hlines Horizontal rule preset string (e.g. "header"). NULL leaves unchanged. vlines Vertical rule preset string (e.g. "box"). NULL leaves unchanged. spacing Named list titles_after, footnotes_before, pagehead_after, pagefoot_before, page_by_after (integer blank lines). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_spacing(). header Named list header defaults. Currently supports: span_gap — Logical. Insert gap columns adjacent spans (default TRUE). See fr_header() details. NULL leaves unchanged. footnote_separator Logical. Whether draw separator footnote block. NULL leaves unchanged.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: follows pattern ggplot2 theme management. Calling fr_theme(...) (alias fr_theme_set(...)) merges new settings current global theme without discarding keys mention. analogous ggplot2::theme_update(). Use fr_theme_reset() clear settings return built-defaults, fr_theme_get() inspect current state.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: fr_table() called, automatically applies stored theme new fr_spec. can still override setting per-table calling corresponding verb fr_table(). Per-table overrides always win. Theme settings stored package's internal environment persist duration R session. Call fr_theme_reset() clear settings fr_theme_get() inspect current theme.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
code: ## ── Define study theme at the top of your program ────────────────────────  fr_theme(   orientation = "landscape",   paper       = "letter",   font_size   = 9,   tokens      = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024"),   pagehead    = list(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ),   pagefoot    = list(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ),   hlines = "header",   vlines = "box" )  # All subsequent fr_table() calls inherit the theme — no need to repeat tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Full Analysis Set") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events", "Safety Analysis Set") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events" #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Set section spacing (blank lines between table parts) ─────────────────  fr_theme(spacing = list(titles_after = 1L, footnotes_before = 1L))  ## ── Merge additional settings (does not discard existing ones) ────────────  fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header") fr_theme(vlines = "box")          # adds vlines; font_size and hlines kept fr_theme_get()                    # inspect #> $orientation #> [1] "landscape" #>  #> $paper #> [1] "letter" #>  #> $font_size #> [1] 9 #>  #> $pagehead #> $pagehead$left #> [1] "Study: {study}" #>  #> $pagehead$right #> [1] "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}" #>  #>  #> $pagefoot #> $pagefoot$left #> [1] "{program}" #>  #> $pagefoot$right #> [1] "{datetime}" #>  #>  #> $tokens #> $tokens$study #> [1] "TFRM-2024-001" #>  #> $tokens$cutoff #> [1] "31DEC2024" #>  #>  #> $hlines #> [1] "header" #>  #> $vlines #> [1] "box" #>  #> $spacing #> $spacing$titles_after #> [1] 1 #>  #> $spacing$footnotes_before #> [1] 1 #>  #>   ## ── Override a theme setting for one specific table ───────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "portrait")   # overrides the landscape theme default #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: portrait letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Theme with spacing configuration ──────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(   font_size = 9,   spacing   = list(titles_after = 2L, footnotes_before = 2L,                    pagehead_after = 1L) ) fr_theme_get()$spacing   # check the spacing settings #> $titles_after #> [1] 2 #>  #> $footnotes_before #> [1] 2 #>  #> $pagehead_after #> [1] 1 #>   ## ── Theme with footnote_separator ────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(footnote_separator = FALSE, hlines = "header") fr_theme_get()$footnote_separator   # FALSE #> [1] FALSE  ## ── Theme auto-applied: inspect spec after fr_table() ────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(font_size = 8, orientation = "portrait", hlines = "header") spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec$page$font_size      # 8 (inherited from theme) #> [1] 8 spec$page$orientation    # "portrait" (inherited from theme) #> [1] "portrait"  ## ── Reset all theme settings ──────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset()

what: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
text: Returns current study-level theme named list. Use inspect defaults set via fr_theme().

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
code: fr_theme_get()

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
text: named list current theme settings, empty list theme set. Possible keys (optional): orientation — "landscape" "portrait" paper — "letter", "a4", "legal" font_family — e.g. "Courier New" font_size — numeric (points) margins — numeric vector (inches) tokens — named list token values pagehead — list left, center, right, font_size, bold pagefoot — list left, center, right, font_size, bold hlines — preset name (e.g. "header") vlines — preset name (e.g. "box") spacing — list titles_after, footnotes_before, pagehead_after, pagefoot_before, page_by_after (integer blank lines) col_gap — integer (points) split — logical (TRUE/FALSE) column splitting stub — character vector (stub column names) footnote_separator — logical

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
code: fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header", orientation = "landscape") fr_theme_get() #> $orientation #> [1] "landscape" #>  #> $font_size #> [1] 9 #>  #> $hlines #> [1] "header" #>  # $font_size # [1] 9 # $hlines # [1] "header" # $orientation # [1] "landscape"  # Programmatic access theme <- fr_theme_get() theme$font_size   # 9 #> [1] 9  ## ── Empty state after reset ───────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme_get()     # list() — no theme settings active #> list() length(fr_theme_get()) == 0L   # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
text: Clears study-level defaults previously set fr_theme(). calling fr_theme_reset(), fr_table() returns built-defaults (YAML config defaults fr_config() loaded). Call test runs, top scripts, switching studies R session.

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
code: fr_theme_reset()

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
code: ## ── Before / after effect ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header", orientation = "landscape") fr_theme_get()$font_size        # 9 #> [1] 9 fr_theme_get()$hlines           # "header" #> [1] "header"  fr_theme_reset()                # clear everything fr_theme_get()                  # list() — empty #> list() length(fr_theme_get()) == 0L    # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  ## ── Reset does not affect YAML config ─────────────────────────────────────  # fr_theme_reset() only clears the session theme. # To also clear YAML config, call fr_config_reset() separately. fr_theme(font_size = 8) fr_theme_reset() fr_theme_get()   # list() #> list()

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: Sets one title lines displayed table body. argument ... produces one title line rendered output. Calling fr_titles() replaces previously set titles. Titles support inline {fr_*()} markup — see fr_bold(), fr_italic(), fr_super(), fr_unicode().

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
code: fr_titles(spec, ..., .align = "center", .bold = FALSE, .font_size = NULL)

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One title lines. may : character scalar — plain text, optionally {fr_*()} markup. list. first element text content (can unnamed). Optionally include align, bold, font_size override per-line styling. .align Default alignment title lines. One "left", "center" (default), "right". Can overridden per line via list form. .bold Default bold title lines. FALSE default. .font_size Default font size points. NULL inherits page font size set fr_page().

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: modified fr_spec. Titles stored spec$meta$titles.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: ICH E3 §10 specifies table carry: Line 1 — Table number title (e.g. "Table 14.1.1 Summary Demographics Baseline Characteristics"). Line 2 — Population qualifier (e.g. "Full Analysis Set" "Safety Population"). Line 3 — Study identifier qualifier (optional, e.g. "Study TFRM-2024-001" "Database Cutoff: 31DEC2024"). Standard table numbering scheme (ICH E3 / FDA/EMA submissions): TFL outputs, titles typically left-aligned, 9 pt, bold. Use "center" alignment PDF journal output.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: default, one blank line inserted last title column header. Control fr_spacing():   can also set _tlframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(titles_after = 0L)   # no gap spec |> fr_spacing(titles_after = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   titles_after: 1

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: Pass 0 arguments (fr_titles(spec)) clear titles. Markup titles: use "{fr_bold('Table 14.1.1')}" bold just table number. glue {...} syntax evaluated spec-build time. fr_titles() always replaces — chain multiple calls want progressively build pipeline, last call takes effect.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
code: ## ── Standard two-line ICH E3 title ──────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Three-line title with study identifier ───────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Analysis Set",     "Study TFRM-2024-001"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (3): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Pre..." #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> 3. [center] "Study TFRM-2024-001" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Inline markup in a title ─────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "{fr_bold('Table 14.1.1')} Summary of Demographics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Per-line styling with list form ──────────────────────────────────────  # List form for per-line styling overrides tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     list("Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics", bold = TRUE, font_size = 10),     list("Full Analysis Set", align = "left", font_size = 9)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [left] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Left-aligned titles (common in pharma RTF) ───────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics",     "Full Analysis Set",     .align = "left"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (2): #> 1. [left] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [left] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Clear all titles ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_titles("Old Title") spec <- spec |> fr_titles()  # removes all titles

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
text: Renders text span underlined final output. Use inside glue-string expressions. Underline less common regulatory tables sometimes used : Confidentiality marks: "CONFIDENTIAL" title headers. Hyperlink-style emphasis electronic submissions. emphasis needs, prefer fr_bold() — standard pharma TFL outputs.

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
code: fr_underline(x)

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
text: x Character. Text underline.

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string, produces sentinel token resolved render time.

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
text: RTF output, underline uses \ul ... \ulnone control words. future LaTeX output, maps \underline{}.

title: Underline Markup — fr_underline
code: # Confidentiality marker fr_underline("CONFIDENTIAL") #> <fr_markup> __CONFIDENTIAL__  # In a title: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_titles("{fr_underline('CONFIDENTIAL')} - Do Not Distribute") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "CONFIDENTIAL - Do Not Distribute" #> Header: valign=bottom

title: Unicode Character by Codepoint — fr_unicode
text: Inserts Unicode character numeric codepoint. render time backend converts appropriate escape sequence (RTF \uN LaTeX command). Use symbols available keyboard cross-platform portability. commonly used symbols clinical tables, prefer dedicated helpers: fr_dagger(), fr_ddagger().

title: Unicode Character by Codepoint — fr_unicode
code: fr_unicode(codepoint)

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). strict Logical. TRUE, validation issue raises error. FALSE (default), issues reported warnings spec returned invisibly.

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: Invisibly returns spec valid (strict = FALSE). Raises error strict mode issues found.

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: Column names fr_cols() exist data page_by, group_by, indent_by, blank_after columns exist stub_cols exist data N-count column names match column specs Span columns exist contiguous Column widths sum printable area (warning >110%) Style row/col indices valid range Font family recognised sort_by / repeat_cols columns exist (listings)

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
code: ## ── Clean spec passes validation silently ──────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header")  spec |> fr_validate()  ## ── Strict mode on a clean spec (no error) ────────────────────────────────  spec |> fr_validate(strict = TRUE)  ## ── Spec with a validation issue: bad column in column specs ────────────────  # Manually inject a column spec for a non-existent column # (fr_cols() validates eagerly, so we set it directly for demo) bad_spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() bad_spec$columns[["nonexistent"]] <- fr_col("Bad Column")  # Non-strict mode: warns but returns the spec bad_spec |> fr_validate() #> Warning: ! 1 validation issue found: #> • Column in `fr_cols()` not found in data: "nonexistent".  # Strict mode errors (wrapped in tryCatch for safety) tryCatch(   bad_spec |> fr_validate(strict = TRUE),   error = function(e) message("Caught: ", conditionMessage(e)) ) #> Caught: ! 1 validation issue found: #> • Column in `fr_cols()` not found in data: "nonexistent".  ## ── Validate before rendering in a pipeline ───────────────────────────────  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "validated.rtf") tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE by SOC") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_validate() |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: Sets vertical rules (column separators) table. Calling fr_vlines() replaces previously set vertical rules. Horizontal rules set fr_hlines() affected.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
code: fr_vlines(   spec,   preset = "box",   cols = NULL,   width = NULL,   color = NULL,   linestyle = NULL,   abovepos = NULL,   belowpos = NULL )

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). preset Named rule preset "void" (vertical rules): "box" — rules leftmost rightmost table edges (outer vertical borders). Default. "" — rules every column outer edges. "inner" — rules columns ; outer edges. "void" — vertical rules. Clears previously set vlines. cols Integer vector column gap positions rule. Position j draws rule right column j. Use alongside preset = "void" add individual column separators. NULL uses preset. width Rule width. Named shorthand ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick") numeric points. Default NULL (thin, 0.5 pt). color Rule colour: hex string CSS named colour. NULL = black. linestyle One "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "dashdot", "double". abovepos, belowpos Fractions (0–1) controlling partial vertical extent rule within row. NULL = full height (default).

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: modified fr_spec. Vertical rules stored spec$rules.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: pharma TFL outputs use vertical rules ("void") just outer box border ("box"). Full grids ("") uncommon regulatory submissions may used dense listing tables shift tables column boundaries aid readability.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: fr_vlines("box") common use — clean outer border. Combine fr_hlines("header") typical pharma style: box outer border + single rule column header. cols = c(1L, 3L) draws rule right columns 1 3 — useful separating stub column data columns. fr_vlines() fr_hlines() independent — manages rules. set , use fr_grid().

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
code: ## ── Common presets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Outer box border only (most common) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Rules between every column tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("all") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Inner column separators only (no outer edges) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("inner") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Remove all vertical rules tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("void") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom column positions ───────────────────────────────────────────────  # Rule after the stub column (column 1) only tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_vlines("void") |>   fr_vlines(cols = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  ## ── Custom width, style, and colour ────────────────────────────────────────  # Thick outer box tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box", width = "thick") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Dashed inner separators tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("all", linestyle = "dashed") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # CSS named colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box", color = "slategray") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  ## ── Combined with fr_hlines ───────────────────────────────────────────────  # Typical regulatory pharma style tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Full grid: all borders tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("hsides") |>   fr_vlines("all") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Courier New #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)

what: tlframe: Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings for Regulatory Submission — tlframe-package
title: tlframe: Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings for Regulatory Submission — tlframe-package
text: Produces regulatory-grade clinical trial tables, listings, figures (TLFs) RTF PDF single specification. Designed pharmaceutical industry workflows support paginated output, spanning headers, decimal alignment, programmatic page headers/footers, company-specific rule presets. Built disciplined S3 classes clean, composable pipeline API. dplyr, tidyr, purrr dependency — lightweight infrastructure packages (rlang, cli, vctrs).

title: tlframe: Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings for Regulatory Submission — tlframe-package
code: tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total", width = 1.5, align = "right")   ) |>   fr_header(     n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),     format = "{name}\n(N={n})",     bold = TRUE   ) |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") |>   fr_page(font_size = 9, orientation = "landscape") |>   fr_pagehead(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1.rtf")

title: tlframe: Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings for Regulatory Submission — tlframe-package
text: fr_ prefix avoids base R clashes (col, text, sub, page) Glue-string markup titles, footnotes, column labels, cell data: "{fr_super(1)} chi-square test" Titles/footnotes always body: page header/footer via fr_pagehead() / fr_pagefoot() {token} placeholders page headers/footers: {thepage}, {total_pages}, {program}, {datetime}, plus custom tokens. Literal brace: {{ produces { output. trimming leading whitespace — pharma indentation preserved Immutable pipeline: every verb returns modified spec, side effects fr_render()
